URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/
TITLE: Boat Rental Marbella 2026: Yachts, Catamarans &amp; Charters
DESCRIPTION: Boat rental in Marbella from €749 for 2h on our 12.5 m flybridge motor yachts (Astondoa 40 + Azimut 39). Departing Puerto Banús. Skipper, fuel, drinks, insurance and VAT included.

We operate a 27-boat fleet in Marbella, led by two 12.5 m flybridge motor yachts — the Astondoa 40 and the Azimut 39 — all departing Puerto Banús with licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, insurance and VAT included. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours and run up to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day . This page is the full overview — fleet, ports, license rules and how to book. Skip the long-form below and message us on WhatsApp for an instant quote with real-time availability across all five ports. Boat rental Marbella prices in 2026 Same boat, same crew, transparent hourly pricing. No high-season uplift, no levante surcharge, no marina fee added at the dock. Duration Price What you get 2 hours €749 Quick spin along the Golden Mile, one swim stop 4 hours €1,299 Half-day — comfortable lunch + swim 6 hours €1,799 Two swim stops, paddleboard, real day out 8 hours €2,299 Full day — Sotogrande or Cabopino itineraries Full grid and per-boat detail at /boats/ . Compare the Astondoa 40 (9 guests, Spanish-built, classic Mediterranean styling) vs the Azimut 39 (11 guests, Italian flybridge, modern lines). Astondoa or Azimut? Both boats are the same length (12.5 m), price (€749 → €2,299), and depart from the same marina (Puerto Banús). What differs: Astondoa 40 — Spanish-built, classic teak and cream interior, up to 9 guests . The "Fufi" you see in our photos. Best for couples and groups up to 8 who want the local Mediterranean feel. Azimut 39 — Italian-built flybridge, modern lines, up to 11 guests . Pick this one for larger groups (9+) or anyone who wants the upper sun-deck experience. Departure ports: Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande Puerto Banús is the default departure for any boat over 12 metres and for everything that calls itself a luxury yacht. It sits 7 km west of Marbella old town, has the deepest berths, and puts the lined-up Ferraris on display as you walk to the pontoon. Marbella Marina (officially Puerto Deportivo Virgen del Carmen) is far more central, walking distance from any old-town hotel, and ideal for guests with mobility constraints. Cabopino, 12 km east, is the quietest pickup point and the closest to the dune beaches; licence-free rentals are concentrated here. Estepona and Sotogrande add another 30 minutes by car each way and are usually only worth it if you're staying there. Our full Puerto Banús vs Marbella Marina comparison covers parking, walking distance and which boats live where. Licence rules you actually need to know You do not need a Spanish boat licence to rent in Marbella as long as either (a) the boat is small enough to fall under the licence-free rules, or (b) the charter comes with a qualified skipper — the case for ~95% of bookings on this site. The licence-free thresholds in Spain are: hull up to 5 metres, motor up to 15 hp, sail up to 6 metres, distance from coast up to 2 nautical miles, daylight hours only, captain at least 18 years old. A quick test before you self-drive: are you confident reversing into a windy berth at Cabopino with a queue of boats waiting? If not, book with a skipper. Full rule details: see our Spain boat licence rules 2026 guide, including what changes if you hold a UK ICC or RYA Day Skipper certificate (you can charter larger boats without a Spanish licence — bring the original certificate, not a copy). What's included — and what is not Standard inclusions on a Marbella skippered charter are the captain, fuel for a normal coastal cruising itinerary, soft drinks and water, ice, light snacks, towels, snorkelling masks and an inflatable donut or paddleboard. What's not included by default: alcohol on board (you can BYO on most boats, ask first), prepared lunch (€25–€60 per head depending on caterer), berth taxes for stops in non-home ports, and tip for the skipper (€20–€50 is normal for a good day, or 10% on luxury charters). Typical itineraries from Marbella Half-day from Puerto Banús: head west past Estepona, stop for a swim at Cala del Faro, optional tapas at Sotogrande, back by sunset. Full-day from Marbella Marina: east towards Cabopino's dunes and snorkel anchorage at Cala Cortés, lunch on board, then ice cream stop in the Cabopino port itself. Two-hour sunset: cruise the Golden Mile coastline at displacement speed, anchor briefly off Nikki Beach, return as the sun drops behind La Concha. A Gibraltar day trip (see our Gibraltar by boat piece) is doable from Sotogrande but adds 80 NM round trip and only makes sense in flat sea. How to book in 60 seconds Three ways: WhatsApp us with your date, group size and rough budget — instant quotes from local operators, no account, no deposit until you confirm. Call if you want to talk through itinerary options first. Browse our 27-boat fleet at /boats/ if you'd rather pick a specific hull yourself. Whichever route you pick, the price you see is what you pay — no booking fees added at checkout, no surprise marina charges. Same advice applies whether you're after a luxury yacht rental Marbella for a corporate day out or a quick 2-hour spin from the central Puerto Banús pontoon. Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella "Every booking on this site lands on my phone first. I run the operations end-to-end with our skippers, so the boat you see is the boat that picks you up — same fleet, same crew, no third-party hand-off." If anything goes sideways before, during or after your charter, I'm the person you message. Most replies under 5 minutes during Marbella daytime. Follow @boatrentalmarbella Frequently asked questions How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella? Boat rental in Marbella on our fleet starts at €749 for 2 hours and runs to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day. Same price across seasons. Every charter includes licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, insurance and Spanish IVA (VAT). For larger groups (9+ guests on Azimut 39) the per-person cost drops to about €70 for the 2-hour cruise. Where do boats depart from in Marbella? The five working pickup points are Puerto Banús (the marquee yacht marina, west of Marbella town), Marbella Marina/Puerto Deportivo Virgen del Carmen (right by the central promenade), Cabopino (eastern Marbella, near the dunes), Estepona, and Sotogrande further west. Puerto Banús handles the largest yachts and most luxury bookings. Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella? No. Spain allows licence-free rentals for boats up to 5 m hull length with engines up to 15 hp (or sail up to 6 m), within 2 nautical miles of the coast, in daylight, captain aged 18 or over. For anything larger, you either hold a PNB/PER licence or — by far the most common option — book a charter with the skipper included. What is included in the price? Every charter on our Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 includes: licensed Spanish skipper, fuel for the standard coastal route, soft drinks and water, snorkel gear, inflatable donut, paddleboard, towels, insurance and Spanish IVA (VAT). Catered lunch (€25–€60 per head) is the main optional add-on. When is the best time to rent a boat in Marbella? June and September are the sweet spot — warm sea (22–24 °C), long sunshine hours, fewer afternoon levante winds than July–August, and prices roughly 10–15% under peak. July and August are busiest and warmest but choppier from 14:00 onwards. April–May and October still deliver flat morning seas at noticeably lower rates. How far in advance should I book? For July, August and any Saturday in summer, book 2–4 weeks ahead — the larger yachts (15 m+) sell out first. For weekday trips in May, June, September or October, 3–5 days is usually enough. For licence-free boats and weekday 2-hour sunset cruises, same-day WhatsApp bookings are routinely possible. Can children come on the boat? Yes — virtually every operator welcomes children. Spanish maritime law requires a correctly-sized life jacket per child on board, which charters provide. The calmest waters for under-5s are typically Marbella Marina to Cabopino in th

TITLE: Bootsverleih Marbella 2026: Yachten, Katamarane &amp; Charter aus Puerto Banús
DESCRIPTION: Bootsverleih in Marbella ab €749 für 2h auf unseren 12,5 m Flybridge-Motoryachten (Astondoa 40 + Azimut 39). Abfahrt Puerto Banús. Skipper, Treibstoff, Getränke, MwSt. inklusive.

Bootsverleih Marbella Bootsverleih in Marbella, betrieben direkt aus Puerto Banús. Wir sind keine Marketplace — jedes Boot auf dieser Seite wird von uns oder unseren Skipper-Partnern selbst betrieben. Skipper, Treibstoff, Getränke (Bier, Weißwein, Cava, Wasser), Snacks, Versicherung und 21% spanische MwSt. sind in jedem Charter enthalten. Preise ab €749 für 2 Stunden bis €2.299 für einen ganzen 8-Stunden-Tag . Unsere Hauptflotte Astondoa 40 'Fufi' — spanische 12,5 m Yacht, bis zu 9 Gäste, klassisches Teakholz-Interieur. €749/2h. Azimut 39 — italienische 12,5 m Flybridge-Yacht, bis zu 11 Gäste, modernes Sonnendeck. €749/2h. Mangusta 80 'Nina' — die größte Charter-Yacht in Marbella, 24 m, inklusive Jetski. €4.719 ab 4h. Was inklusive ist Lizenzierter Skipper · Treibstoff für die Standard-Küstenroute · Bier · Weißwein · Cava · alkoholfreie Getränke · Wasser · Eis · leichte Snacks · Schnorchelausrüstung · Donut · Paddleboard · Handtücher · Versicherung · Spanische IVA (MwSt. 21%). Abfahrtshäfen Puerto Banús ist der Hauptabfahrtshafen — die meisten Yachten ab 12 m starten hier. Alternative Pickup-Punkte: Marbella Marina (zentral), Cabopino (östlich, dünenfreundlich), Estepona und Sotogrande auf Anfrage. Siehe Bootsverleih Puerto Banús für Details zum Hauptmarina. Buchung in 60 Sekunden WhatsApp an Andra mit Datum, Gruppengröße und Budget — sofortiges Angebot. 50% Anzahlung sichert das Datum, Rest am Tag des Charters. Skipper holt euch ab in Puerto Banús — fertig. Tippt auf den Buchen -Button oben oder direkt auf WhatsApp schreiben . Lizenzregeln in Spanien Du brauchst keinen spanischen Bootsführerschein , wenn (a) das Boot unter 5 m Länge / 15 PS bleibt (führerscheinfrei), oder (b) du mit Skipper buchst — was bei ~95% unserer Charters der Fall ist. Wenn du eine deutsche SBF-See oder höher hast, kannst du bestimmte größere Boote selbst steuern — bring das Original-Zertifikat mit. Häufige Fragen Wie viel kostet ein Boot in Marbella? Ab €749 für 2 Stunden auf unseren 12,5 m Yachten (Astondoa oder Azimut), bis €2.299 für einen ganzen 8-Stunden-Tag. Skipper, Treibstoff, Getränke, MwSt. und Versicherung inklusive — kein Aufpreis am Hafen. Brauche ich einen Bootsführerschein? Nein — alle skipperten Charters auf dieser Seite sind führerscheinfrei für dich. Du kannst dich entspannt zurücklehnen. Was ist die beste Reisezeit? Juni und September sind ideal — warmes Meerwasser (22-24°C), wenig Wind, etwas günstiger als Hochsaison. Juli/August sind voll und manchmal windig am Nachmittag. Kann ich auf Deutsch reservieren? Ja — Andra antwortet auf Deutsch, Englisch, Spanisch und Estnisch. Schreib einfach auf WhatsApp. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verifizierte Bewertungen Ab €749 2h Charter mit Skipper WhatsApp jetzt — durchschnittliche Antwort in unter 5 Minuten. Keine Anzahlung bis zur Bestätigung. 💬 Auf WhatsApp schreiben Ø Antwort < 5 Min · keine Anzahlung bis zur Bestätigung 📅 Jetzt buchen Ganze Flotte ansehen → In jedem Charter enthalten Lizenzierter Skipper & Treibstoff Getränke: Wasser, alkoholfreie Drinks, Bier, Weißwein, Cava Leichte Snacks Versicherung & Sicherheitsausrüstung Schnorchelausrüstung & Wasserspielzeug 21% MwSt. — keine Überraschungen

TITLE: Alquiler de barcos en Marbella 2026: Yates desde €749
DESCRIPTION: Alquiler de barcos en Marbella desde €749/2h en nuestros yates a motor de 12,5 m (Astondoa 40 y Azimut 39). Salida desde Puerto Banús — patrón, combustible, bebidas, snacks e IVA incluidos.

Inicio Operamos tres yates en Marbella — el Astondoa 40 , el Azimut 39 y el Mangusta 80 , todos con salida desde Puerto Banús y con patrón profesional, combustible, bebidas, snacks, seguro e IVA incluidos. Los precios arrancan en €749 por 2 horas y llegan hasta €4.719 por 4 horas en el Mangusta 80 de lujo (incluye moto de agua). Precios de alquiler de barcos en Marbella 2026 Misma flota, misma tripulación, precio transparente por hora. Sin recargo de temporada alta, sin recargo de levante, sin tasas portuarias añadidas en el muelle. Duración Precio Qué incluye 2 horas €749 Vuelta rápida por la Milla de Oro, una parada de baño 4 horas €1.299 Medio día — comida y baño cómodos 6 horas €1.799 Dos paradas de baño, paddleboard, día completo 8 horas €2.299 Día completo — itinerarios hasta Sotogrande o Cabopino Tarifa por hora completa y detalle por barco en /boats/ . Compara el Astondoa 40 (9 invitados, fabricado en España, estilo clásico mediterráneo) con el Azimut 39 (11 invitados, flybridge italiano, líneas modernas). ¿Astondoa o Azimut? Ambos barcos miden lo mismo (12,5 m), tienen el mismo precio (€749 → €2.299) y salen del mismo puerto (Puerto Banús). En qué se diferencian: Astondoa 40 — fabricado en Cádiz, interior clásico en teca y crema, hasta 9 invitados . El "Fufi" que ves en nuestras fotos. Ideal para parejas y grupos hasta 8 que buscan un toque mediterráneo local. Azimut 39 — flybridge italiano, líneas modernas, hasta 11 invitados . La opción para grupos más grandes (9+) o quien quiera disfrutar de la cubierta superior con tumbonas al sol. Nuestra flota Yates para reservar hoy Los tres yates de nuestra flota en Puerto Banús — elige uno, escríbenos por WhatsApp y reservamos en 60 segundos. Desde €4719 4h con patrón Ver yate → Ver toda la flota → Salidas desde Puerto Banús Toda nuestra flota atraca en Puerto Banús — el puerto más profundo y emblemático de la Costa del Sol. Te enviamos el número de pantalán y amarre 24 horas antes. Aparcamiento subterráneo a 5 minutos del muelle. Para más detalle, consulta nuestra guía de la flota . Itinerarios típicos desde Marbella Oeste (Milla de Oro y Estepona): salida desde Puerto Banús, rumbo oeste pasando frente a Marbella Club, Puente Romano y Nikki Beach. Fondeamos en Cala del Faro para baño y snorkel. Vuelta con vistas al atardecer sobre Gibraltar en días claros. Este (Cabopino y dunas): rumbo este por la Milla de Oro, fondeo en Río Real o Cabopino. Aguas más calmadas por las mañanas y excelente para grupos con niños. Ver nuestra guía de barcos con niños (en inglés). Atardecer (2 h): salida 75 minutos antes del ocaso. Cruzamos la Milla de Oro a velocidad lenta, fondeo breve frente a Río Verde y vuelta con el sol cayendo tras La Concha. La opción más romántica para parejas. Por qué reservar con nosotros Flota propia: tres yates en propiedad y mantenimiento directo. No somos intermediarios — eres tú quien sube a nuestros barcos. Patrones locales: nuestros capitanes conocen cada cala, viento y horario portuario de la Costa del Sol. Sin coste oculto: el precio que ves incluye combustible, bebidas (cerveza, vino blanco, cava), seguro e IVA. Cero sorpresas al pagar. Reserva flexible: 50% al reservar, resto la mañana del charter. Reembolso completo hasta 7 días antes. Mal tiempo: 100% reembolso siempre. Respuesta inmediata: WhatsApp atendido por personas reales — respuesta media en menos de 5 minutos. Qué incluye cada alquiler Patrón con titulación profesional para toda la duración Bebidas a bordo: agua, refrescos, cerveza, vino blanco y cava Snacks ligeros (fruta, patatas, almendras, galletas) Combustible para la ruta costera estándar Seguro y equipo de seguridad completo (chalecos, bengalas, botiquín) IVA español (21%) — sin sorpresas al pagar Equipo de snorkel y juguetes inflables (donut, paddleboard) Comida cocinada, bebidas premium, DJ y tender a chiringuito son extras que puedes añadir al reservar. Reserva en 60 segundos Escríbenos por WhatsApp con tu fecha, número de invitados y presupuesto aproximado. Te respondemos con cotización al momento, sin compromiso. 50% al reservar para asegurar la fecha, el resto la mañana del charter. Cancelación gratuita hasta 7 días antes. Cancelación por mal tiempo siempre con reembolso del 100%. Preguntas frecuentes ¿Cuánto cuesta alquilar un barco en Marbella? El alquiler de barcos en Marbella en nuestra flota arranca en €749 por 2 horas y llega hasta €2.299 por un día completo de 8 horas. Mismo precio en cualquier temporada. Cada alquiler incluye patrón con titulación, combustible, bebidas, seguro e IVA. Para grupos grandes (11 invitados en el Azimut 39), el coste por persona baja a unos €70 para 2 horas. ¿Dónde sale el barco? Toda nuestra flota sale desde Puerto Banús — el puerto deportivo más profundo de Marbella y con la mayor concentración de yates de la Costa del Sol. Te enviamos el número de pantalán y amarre 24 horas antes de la salida. ¿Necesito licencia náutica? No. Nuestros barcos llevan patrón con titulación profesional incluido. Tú y tus invitados sois pasajeros — no necesitas certificación ninguna, solo DNI o pasaporte para subir a bordo. ¿Qué pasa si hace mal tiempo? El patrón confirma la noche anterior. Si el viento previsto supera la fuerza 4-5 (~20+ nudos) o el estado del mar hace el viaje incómodo, se reprograma sin coste o se hace reembolso del 100%. Lluvia ligera por sí sola no es motivo de cancelación en la Costa del Sol. Con nuestros clientes a bordo Charters reales con grupos reales en Marbella, Puerto Banús y la Costa del Sol. URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/astondoa-40-vs-pershing-46-marbella-comparison/
TITLE: Astondoa 40 vs Pershing 46: Which Yacht Suits Your Marbella Charter?
DESCRIPTION: Compare Astondoa 40 vs Pershing 46 for your Marbella charter. See specs, capacity, pricing, and which suits your group. Book now.

Home › Astondoa 40 vs Pershing 46: Which Yacht Suits Your Marbella Charter? Choosing between the Astondoa 40 and Pershing 46 for your Marbella charter? Both depart from Puerto Banús and offer premium experiences, but they suit different budgets and group sizes. This comparison breaks down the key differences in size, capacity, pricing, and inclusions to help you decide which yacht fits your day on the Costa del Sol. Size and Capacity The Astondoa 40 measures 12.5 metres and accommodates up to 9 passengers. The Pershing 46 is larger at 14 metres, with a maximum capacity of 10 guests. For groups of 10, the Pershing is the only option; for 9 or fewer, the Astondoa offers ample space. Both yachts provide sunbathing areas, a saloon, and a small galley, but the Pershing's extra length gives slightly more deck room. Pricing and Value The Astondoa 40 has transparent pricing: €749 for 2 hours, scaling up to €2,299 for 8 hours. This includes a skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, insurance, and Spanish IVA. The Pershing 46 is priced on request via WhatsApp, making it less straightforward for budget planning but potentially negotiable for longer charters. For cost certainty, the Astondoa 40 wins. Inclusions and Amenities Both charters include the same standard package: licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and 21% IVA. No hidden extras. The Pershing 46 may offer more premium finishes, but the core inclusions are identical. You can bring your own food on either yacht, and catering is available on request. Performance and Style The Pershing 46 is known for sporty lines and higher performance, appealing to those who want a faster, more dynamic ride. The Astondoa 40 is a classic flybridge cruiser, prioritising comfort and stability at cruising speeds. Both are well-maintained and depart from Puerto Banús, giving you direct access to Marbella Bay, Cabopino, and Estepona. Ideal Use Cases Choose the Astondoa 40 if: you have 9 or fewer guests, want fixed pricing from €749, and prefer a simple, all-inclusive booking. Perfect for a half-day trip to Cala del Faro or a sunset cruise along the Golden Mile. Choose the Pershing 46 if: your group is 10 people, you value a sportier design, and you're flexible on budget. Ideal for full-day excursions to Sotogrande or longer coastal tours. Which Departs from Puerto Banús? Both yachts operate from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. This means easy access to restaurants, shops, and parking. Whether you choose the Astondoa 40 or Pershing 46, you'll start your charter from the heart of the Costa del Sol's luxury scene. Explore our full fleet for more options. Frequently asked questions Which is bigger, Astondoa 40 or Pershing 46? The Pershing 46 is larger at 14 metres (46 ft) compared to the Astondoa 40 at 12.5 metres (40 ft). The Pershing offers more deck space and a higher passenger capacity (10 vs 9). Can I book the Pershing 46 for a 2-hour trip? Pricing for the Pershing 46 is on request, so you can ask for shorter durations. However, the Astondoa 40 has a fixed rate of €749 for 2 hours, making it a more straightforward option for quick trips. What's included in the Astondoa 40 charter price? The Astondoa 40 rate includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs. Which yacht is better for a group of 10? The Pershing 46 can take up to 10 passengers, while the Astondoa 40 is capped at 9. For a full group of 10, choose the Pershing 46. For 9 or fewer, the Astondoa 40 works well. Do both yachts depart from the same marina? Yes, both the Astondoa 40 and Pershing 46 depart from Puerto Banús in Marbella. This makes them equally convenient for exploring the Costa del Sol coastline. Is the Pershing 46 faster than the Astondoa 40? Both are motor yachts, but exact speeds are not specified. The Pershing 46 is a performance-oriented model known for speed, while the Astondoa 40 offers a comfortable cruise. Contact us for specific performance details. Can I bring my own food on either yacht? Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food. Each charter includes complimentary drinks and light snacks. Catering can also be arranged on request for both yachts. How do I book the Pershing 46? To book the Pershing 46, contact us via WhatsApp for pricing and availability. The Astondoa 40 can be booked directly online with fixed rates starting at €749 for 2 hours. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/azimut-58-vs-sunseeker-predator-74-marbella/
TITLE: Azimut 58 vs Sunseeker Predator 74: Marbella Yacht Showdown
DESCRIPTION: Azimut 58 vs Sunseeker Predator 74 Marbella: compare size, speed, price &amp; luxury. Which yacht suits your charter? Book your day from Puerto Banús.

Home › Azimut 58 vs Sunseeker Predator 74: Marbella Yacht Showdown Choosing between the Azimut 58 Flybridge and the Sunseeker Predator 74 for your Marbella yacht charter? Both are luxury powerboats, but they cater to different tastes. The Azimut 58 (17.6m, 12 pax) is a flybridge cruiser perfect for lounging and socialising, while the Predator 74 (22.5m, 12 pax) is a sportier open yacht built for speed. This guide breaks down the differences in size, speed, price, and local cruising experience to help you decide. We operate the Azimut 58 from Puerto Banús—the Predator 74 is available via other operators but we’ll compare them fairly. For the full range of boats available, browse our boat rental Marbella hub. Size and Layout The Azimut 58 Flybridge measures 17.6 metres and features a spacious flybridge with a sunpad, dining area, and steering station. Below deck, you’ll find three cabins and two heads. The Predator 74 is larger at 22.5 metres, with a more open-plan layout—no flybridge, but a vast cockpit and foredeck sunbathing area. The Predator’s extra length gives more interior volume, often with four cabins. For groups prioritising al fresco lounging, the Azimut 58’s flybridge is a clear advantage. Performance and Speed The Sunseeker Predator 74 is built for thrill-seekers, with twin MAN engines pushing it to 38–40 knots. The Azimut 58 Flybridge cruises at 22–26 knots, which is ample for reaching Cala del Faro or Estepona in 30–40 minutes. If you want to feel the wind in your hair and cover more coastline quickly, the Predator wins. For a relaxed cruise with drinks and conversation, the Azimut is the better choice. Pricing Comparison Our Azimut 58 Flybridge pricing is on request via WhatsApp, but expect €2,500–€4,000 for a half-day (4–5 hours) and up to €6,000 for a full day, all inclusive. The Sunseeker Predator 74 typically starts at €5,000 for a half-day and can exceed €8,000 for a full day, due to its larger size and higher fuel consumption. Both include a skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. For budget-conscious groups, the Azimut 58 offers excellent value without sacrificing luxury. Local Cruising: Marbella to Sotogrande Departing Puerto Banús , both yachts can explore the same iconic stops: Marbella Marina , Cabopino , Cala Cortés , and Sotogrande . The Azimut 58’s flybridge is perfect for spotting La Concha mountain while anchored. The Predator’s open bow allows for sunbathing at Río Verde . For a longer cruise to Estepona (about 10 NM), the Predator gets you there faster, but the Azimut offers a more leisurely journey with better social spaces. Which Yacht Should You Choose? Choose the Azimut 58 Flybridge if you value comfort, social lounging, and a flybridge view. It’s ideal for families or groups wanting a relaxed day with dining, swimming, and sunbathing. Choose the Sunseeker Predator 74 if you want a sportier, faster experience and don’t mind a more open layout. If you’re set on the Azimut 58, book the Azimut 58 Flybridge directly from our fleet. For other options, see our full fleet . Frequently asked questions Which is faster, Azimut 58 or Sunseeker Predator 74? The Sunseeker Predator 74 typically reaches 38–40 knots, while the Azimut 58 Flybridge cruises at 22–26 knots. The Predator is built for speed; the Azimut for cruising comfort. Can I charter a Sunseeker Predator 74 in Marbella? Yes, through other operators. Our fleet does not include a Predator 74, but we offer the Azimut 58 Flybridge, which provides a similar luxury experience from Puerto Banús. How many people can the Azimut 58 carry? The Azimut 58 Flybridge accommodates up to 12 passengers, making it ideal for groups of friends or family. The Sunseeker Predator 74 also holds 12 pax. What is the price difference between Azimut 58 and Predator 74? Our Azimut 58 pricing is on request via WhatsApp, typically €2,500–€4,000 for a half-day. The Predator 74 generally costs €5,000–€8,000+ per day, reflecting its larger size and higher speed. Which yacht is better for a relaxing day in Marbella? The Azimut 58 Flybridge, with its spacious deck and flybridge lounging, is perfect for relaxing. The Predator 74 is better for those wanting a sportier ride and faster coastal hops. Where do charters depart from? Both the Azimut 58 (our boat) and most Predator 74 charters in Marbella depart from Puerto Banús, the main luxury marina on the Costa del Sol. What is included with the Azimut 58 charter? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Can I anchor for swimming on either yacht? Yes, both yachts can anchor at spots like Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or off Cabopino. The Azimut 58’s flybridge offers great views while swimming. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

TITLE: Boat Party Marbella 2026: Stag, Hen &amp; Group Charters
DESCRIPTION: Boat party Marbella for groups 9–11 on our Azimut 39 from €749/2h or €2,299/full day. Stag, hen, birthday or corporate. BYO welcomed, DJ add-on available. Departing Puerto Banús.

Home › Boat Party Marbella 2026: Stag, Hen & Group Charters Best fit for groups: the Azimut 39 takes up to 11 guests — perfect for stag, hen and birthday groups. Full day (8 h) is €2,299 , which works out to about €210 per person on a full boat. BYO welcomed, DJ + speaker upgrade available. Marbella's party-yacht scene is built around the 14:00–18:00 slot in July and August. You board with the group, the skipper runs west along the Golden Mile, drops anchor at Río Verde or Río Real for a swim-and-music stop, optionally tenders the group to Nikki Beach for a paid entry, then returns just in time to clean up and hit the marina restaurants. Prices start at €1,200 for a half-day private charter. Boat party Marbella prices 2026 Boat Guests 4 h half-day Full day 8 h 16-18 m motor yacht up to 12-16 €1,200-1,800 €2,200-2,800 45 ft party catamaran up to 16-20 €1,400-1,900 €2,400-3,200 22 m+ luxury yacht up to 12 (day) €2,200-2,800 €3,200-4,500 50 ft commercial catamaran up to 30 €1,900-2,400 €3,200-4,000 Commercial party boat up to 40 €1,500-2,200 €3,000-4,200 Prices include skipper, crew where applicable, fuel for a standard 12 NM coastal cruise, ice tubs, Bluetooth speaker and standard inflatables. For broader pricing context across all boat types, see how much it costs to rent a boat in Marbella . Standard 4-hour party itinerary 14:00 Boarding at Puerto Banús, ice loaded, drinks chilled, BYO stowed. 14:30 Cast off, cruise west towards Estepona along the Golden Mile. 15:15 Anchor at Río Verde or off Marbella Club, swim ladder down, donut and SUP launched. 15:45 Skipper opens the music — DJ takes over if booked. 16:30 Optional tender to a beach club, or push further east to Cabopino. 17:30 Cruise back to Puerto Banús, slow lap of the harbour mouth for photos. 18:00 Disembark, taxis or walk to dinner. Stag and hen specifics Stag and hen weekends in Marbella usually combine the boat party with one of three add-ons: Jet-ski session tied to the charter — skipper coordinates pickup, €60–€90/person. Beach club entry at Nikki Beach, Ocean Club or Sala Beach — the boat tenders the group ashore, you spend 90 minutes, then re-board. Beach club entry is separate (€50–€150 minimum spend per head). Catering on board — paella party, sushi platter or full hot lunch. €30–€80 per head, 24 h notice. For a quieter group experience that's more day-out than party, the yacht charter Marbella page covers standard private charters. What you can and can't do Yes: BYO alcohol on private charters, loud music at anchor outside marina limits, jumping off the boat at the anchor stop, costume changes on board, inflatable swans, drone photography (your own pilot). No: glass on deck (cans and plastic only on most boats), confetti or anything that ends up in the water (Spanish marina fines are punishing), open flames (no shisha or candles), drugs (the skipper will return to port and the deposit is forfeit). Spanish maritime alcohol law applies to the skipper, not guests — but be sensible. Best months for a Marbella boat party Late June through early September is peak — water at 22–24 °C, long daylight, the marina scene at full volume. July is the busiest single month. Mid-September keeps the warmth and drops the price 10–15%. May, June and October work for groups happy with 19–21 °C water (swims are shorter but everything else still flies). Outside that window party charters still run but the on-deck temperature won't sustain a long anchor stop. Booking a boat party Send a WhatsApp with: date, group size, hen/stag/birthday/corporate, half-day vs full-day, BYO or paid bar preference, and whether you want DJ and beach-club tender included. We send back 2–3 specific boats that fit, with photos, exact pier number at Puerto Banús, and an itemised quote. Book 3–4 weeks ahead for July/August Saturday slots; weekday availability is reasonable up to 5–7 days out. Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. I run the operations end-to-end with our skippers, so the boat you see is the boat that picks you up — same fleet, same crew, no third-party hand-off." If anything goes sideways before, during or after your charter, I'm the person you message. Most replies under 5 minutes during Marbella daytime. Follow @boatrentalmarbella Frequently asked questions How much is a boat party in Marbella? Half-day for 16 guests on a 16 m yacht costs €1,200–€1,800 (€75–€110 per person). Full day on a 22 m party yacht for 25 guests €2,800–€4,500. Larger commercial party boats up to 40 guests from €1,500 for a half-day. What size group fits on which boat? Up to 8: 10–12 m motor yacht. 10–16: 16–18 m yacht or 45 ft catamaran. 18–25: 22 m+ yacht or 50 ft commercial catamaran. 25–40: dedicated party boat. Above 40: split into two boats. Can we BYO alcohol? Yes on private charters — most operators welcome BYO and provide ice. Cava, prosecco and spirits all fine; cans/plastic only on deck. What about DJ and music? Every charter has Bluetooth speakers. Add pro DJ + dedicated system for €350 half-day or €600 full-day. Live saxophonists €400–€600. Are stag/hen activities included? Customisable: jet-skis €60/person, beach club tender (entry separate), catering €30–€80 per head with 24 h notice, photographer €200/hour. We coordinate via WhatsApp. On the water with us Real charters with real groups across Marbella, Puerto Banús and the Costa del Sol. TITLE: Boat Rental No License Marbella: Prices and Options
DESCRIPTION: Boat rental no license Marbella prices: from €749/2h for a 12.5m Astondoa 40. Licensed skipper, drinks &amp; snacks included. Book your no-license charter today.

Home › Boat Rental No License Marbella: Prices and Options Planning a day on the water in Marbella but don't hold a boating license? You can still enjoy a luxury yacht charter without any paperwork. Our boat rental no license Marbella prices start from €749 for 2 hours on a 12.5m Astondoa 40, with a licensed skipper included. Every charter covers fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT – so you just turn up and relax. Whether you want a short spin past the Golden Mile or a full day cruising to Estepona, we have the right boat for you. No License Required – How It Works Spanish law requires a license for private boat operation, but our charters bypass that entirely. Every boat in our fleet comes with a professional skipper who handles all navigation, mooring, and safety. You are free to swim, sunbathe, and enjoy the views. The skipper also acts as your local guide, pointing out landmarks like La Concha mountain, Río Verde, and the exclusive villas along the coast. No experience, no exam – just book and go. Boat Rental No License Marbella Prices 2025 We offer transparent pricing with no surprises. Here are the rates for our most popular no-license charters: Boat Length Capacity 2 Hours 8 Hours Astondoa 40 12.5 m 9 pax €749 €2,299 Azimut 39 12.5 m 11 pax €749 €2,299 Mangusta 80 24 m 12 pax €4,719 (min 4h) – All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. Larger yachts like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86 are available on request – contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote. For a full list of options, visit our boat rental Marbella homepage . From the compact 6.4m Mariah SX21 (5 pax) to the impressive 29m Ferretti 94 (12 pax), you can choose based on group size and budget. Popular choices for no-license rentals include the Azimut 58 Flybridge for its spacious deck and the Mangusta 80 for its speed and jet ski add-on. Licensed skipper (handles all navigation) Fuel for the entire trip Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava Light snacks (chips, olives, nuts) Full insurance and safety equipment Spanish IVA (21% VAT) – no hidden taxes You can also bring your own food and drinks. Just let us know in advance so we can prepare the onboard facilities. Best Routes for No-License Charters From Puerto Banús, your skipper can take you to the iconic Marbella Marina, the quiet coves of Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, or further west to Estepona and Sotogrande. On a 4-hour trip, you can anchor for swimming and lunch near Cabopino. For a full day, head to the pristine waters of the Costa del Sol or even cross to the African coast on longer charters (subject to weather). Your skipper will tailor the route to your preferences. How to Book Your No-License Boat Booking is simple. Browse our fleet online, choose your preferred boat and duration, then contact us via WhatsApp or the booking form. We recommend booking at least 48 hours ahead, especially in summer. Payment can be made by bank transfer or card. For custom requests – like a specific departure time or catering – just ask. Visit our Marbella boat rental page to get started. Frequently Asked Questions Do I need a license to rent a boat in Marbella? No, you do not need a boating license for any of our charters. Every boat comes with a licensed skipper who handles navigation, mooring, and safety. You simply relax and enjoy the coast. How much does a no-license boat rental in Marbella cost? Our most affordable no-license options start at €749 for 2 hours on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39. For longer trips, the same boats cost €2,299 for 8 hours. Larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 start from €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours. What is included in the rental price? The price includes a professional skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, full insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). There are no extra charges. Can I bring my own food and drinks? Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. Our boats have coolers and onboard facilities. Just let us know in advance so we can prepare the space. How many people can I take on a no-license boat? Capacity varies by boat. The smallest boats (Dubhe, Mariah SX21) take up to 5 passengers, while larger yachts like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86 accommodate up to 12 passengers. Check the specific boat page for exact numbers. Which destinations can we visit without a license? You can explore Puerto Banús, the Marbella coastline, Cabopino, Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and even Estepona or Sotogrande on longer charters. Your skipper will suggest the best route based on weather and your preferences. How do I book a no-license boat rental in Marbella? You can book directly through our website or contact us via WhatsApp for availability and custom requests. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially for weekends and peak season. What happens if the weather is bad? Your safety is our priority. If the weather is unsafe for sailing, we will offer you a full refund or reschedule to another date at no extra cost. URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/boat-rental-no-license-marbella/
TITLE: Boat Rental Marbella No License 2026: Self-Drive 5m / 15hp Boats
DESCRIPTION: License-free boat rules in Spain (5m / 15hp / 2 NM). Our skippered fleet starts at €749 — no license needed because we provide the captain. Full guide to no-license boating in Marbella.

Home › Boat Rental Marbella No License 2026: Self-Drive 5m / 15hp Boats Skip the licence question: our fleet ( Astondoa 40 , Azimut 39 ) is crewed by a licensed skipper, so you don't need any license at all. From €749 for 2 h , group capacity 9–11 guests. The page below covers the licence-free rules if you want to drive a smaller boat yourself. The short version: yes, you can rent a boat in Marbella with no licence, no certificate, and no formal sailing experience — as long as you stick to the Spanish rule set of 5 metres hull, 15 horsepower engine, 2 nautical miles from the coast, daylight only, and you're at least 18 years old. From €130 for 2 hours, this is the cheapest and most flexible way to get on the water on the Costa del Sol. The Spanish no-licence rules (2026) Spain's leisure boating regulation 875/2014 defines the licence-free category — known officially as navegación sin titulación — with the following limits: Hull length: 5 metres maximum for motor boats, 6 metres for sail-only boats. Engine power: 15 hp maximum (no exceptions — operators are checked). Distance from coast: 2 nautical miles (~3.7 km). Most coastal anchorages from Marbella sit well inside this. Time of day: daylight only. You must return before sunset. Captain age: 18 years or over with a valid ID. Alcohol: the same blood-alcohol limit as driving applies to the helm operator. For the broader licensing picture (and what you can charter if you have a foreign certificate), see our Spain boat licence rules guide. Where to pick up your no-licence boat Three Marbella locations operate licence-free fleets: Cabopino — biggest no-licence fleet on this stretch of coast. Easiest exit (the harbour mouth is wide and the immediate water is shallow and forgiving). Most affordable. Puerto Banús — fewer no-licence operators, slightly higher prices, but a great location if you want to anchor off Nikki Beach for the afternoon. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) — small no-licence fleet, central location, easy parking nearby. If you're staying further west, Estepona has additional licence-free operators that are 15–20% cheaper than Puerto Banús for the same boat. Skip the licence — book skippered If you want the simpler path: book our crewed fleet. Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 run €749 for 2 h with the skipper included — no licence, no exam, no on-the-dock briefing. The page below explains the official Spanish licence-free rules for context, but they only apply if you want to drive a small (under 5 m / 15 hp) self-drive boat yourself. What to expect — your first time at the helm Arrive 30 minutes before departure. You'll show ID and credit card, sign a one-page rental contract, and the operator walks you through: Throttle and gear shift. One lever — forward, neutral, reverse. Outboards on 15 hp boats are simple. Steering. Either a wheel or, on the smallest boats, a tiller. Anchor. How to drop it and how to retrieve it without trapping fingers. VHF radio. Channel 16 is emergency; you'll rarely need it within 2 NM. Coastal exclusion zones. The 200 m swimmer buoy zone along every Marbella beach — you cannot cross it under power. Refuel procedure. Where the fuel dock is, payment method. The whole briefing takes 10–15 minutes. Then you take the boat for a controlled lap of the marina with the operator watching from the dock, and after that you're on your own. Good itineraries for a 4-hour licence-free trip From Cabopino: head west 20 minutes to anchor off the Cabopino dune beach, swim and snorkel for an hour, push west another 15 minutes to Río Real, swim again, return to Cabopino with 30 minutes of buffer. Stays inside 2 NM the whole time. From Puerto Banús: head east 25 minutes to anchor off Nikki Beach (water is deeper here, anchor sets cleanly), swim, optional tender visit, return via the Golden Mile coastline so you see the famous waterfront mansions. Easy first-timer route. Is a no-licence boat right for you? Yes if: you're a group of 2–4, you want flexibility on timing, you'd rather drive yourself than be a passenger, and you're comfortable with light wind chop. No if: you're 6+ people (capacity is 4–5 max), you want speed (15 hp is slow), you want range (2 NM is tight), or you don't enjoy thinking about wind and tide. For larger groups, look at yacht charter Marbella with a skipper, or compare all options on the boat rental Marbella hub. Frequently asked questions Can I really rent a boat in Marbella without a licence? Yes. Spanish maritime law permits any adult over 18 to operate a boat up to 5 metres long with an engine up to 15 hp (or a sailboat up to 6 m), within 2 nautical miles of the coast, in daylight, with no licence and no formal training. You'll do a 10-minute on-the-dock briefing with the operator covering throttle, anchor and VHF radio, then you're on your way. What can a 15 hp boat actually do? A 5 m / 15 hp boat in Marbella cruises at 8–12 knots fully loaded with 4 adults, planes lightly when solo or with two. You'll comfortably reach the anchor spots off Marbella Marina, Río Verde or the Cabopino dunes within 20–30 minutes. Estepona and Puerto Banús-to-Cabopino in a day is realistic. How much is a no-license boat rental in Marbella? €130 for 2 hours in low season (October to May), €180 in high season (June to September). Full half-day (4 hours) is €220–€280. Full day (8 hours) is €380–€450. Most operators take a refundable security deposit of €200–€300. Is fuel included? Practice varies. About half of Marbella operators include fuel in the rental price; the other half operate on 'return with the tank as you found it'. Either way, a 15 hp engine burns very little — a 2-hour cruise typically uses €8–€15 of petrol. What happens if I damage the boat? Standard policy: the security deposit covers minor damage. For anything larger, every license-free rental in Spain comes with mandatory third-party liability insurance, which the operator carries. You're not personally liable beyond the deposit unless you've been reckless or drunk at the helm. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/boat-rental-puerto-banus/
TITLE: Boat Rental Puerto Banús 2026: Marbella&#x27;s #1 Marina
DESCRIPTION: Boat rental Puerto Banús from €749 for 2h. Our 12.5 m flybridge yachts (Astondoa 40 + Azimut 39) crewed and departing Pier 3 — skipper, fuel, drinks and VAT included.

Home › Boat Rental Puerto Banús 2026: Marbella's #1 Marina Puerto Banús is the marina you've already seen in Instagram reels — superyachts moored stern-to along Pier 1, Lamborghinis parked nose-out on Calle Ribera, and the Bocaccio nightclub a 90-second walk from the pontoon. It's also the practical centre of Marbella yacht charter: roughly 60% of bookable inventory on the Costa del Sol berths here. Boat rental from Puerto Banús starts at €250 for a 2-hour skippered trip. Why Puerto Banús dominates Marbella boat rental Three reasons it's the default departure port: Depth and berth size. Puerto Banús has 915 berths and the deepest harbour mouth on the Marbella stretch — the only marina locally that can routinely accommodate yachts over 30 metres. If you want a true luxury charter, you'll be here. Fleet concentration. Sixty per cent of charter inventory between Estepona and Cabopino is moored at Puerto Banús. Catamarans, sportfishers, party yachts and crewed superyachts cluster here. Marina infrastructure. Refuelling, fresh water, electrical hook-up, marine chandlers and ten dockside restaurants for pre-boarding lunch — Puerto Banús has all of it within a 5-minute walk. Puerto Banús vs Marbella Marina breaks down the trade-offs for non-Banús options. Where to meet the boat Puerto Banús is laid out as eight long parallel pontoons running south-east into the harbour, numbered 1 through 8 from west to east: Pier Typical fleet Walk from car park 1 Superyachts 30 m+, charter brokers 3 min 2–3 Luxury motor yachts 14–22 m 5 min 4–5 Motor yachts 10–14 m, catamarans 6 min 6 Sportfishers, sailing yachts 7 min 7 Day boats, licence-free, small sailboats 8 min 8 Working fleet, fuel dock 10 min Your charter will send you the exact pier and slip number 24 hours before. Show up 15 minutes early — Mediterranean charters leave on time, and missing the slot in summer often means losing the booking. Parking, food and pre-board essentials Parking: Aparcamiento Puerto Banús (underground) is the only sane option in July and August. €2/hour, €18 for the day. Free street parking exists west of the marina but never works mid-summer. Quick lunch before boarding: Antonio's Beach Club (tapas, walking distance), Picasso (Spanish, faster service), or grab a bocadillo from the SuperSol on Calle Ribera. Forgot something: Galerías El Corte Inglés (5-minute walk west) for sunscreen, swimwear, hats, electronics, and a basic pharmacy. Puerto Banús boat rental prices 2026 Boat 2 h 4 h Full day Guests License-free 5 m €180 €280 €450 4-5 Motor yacht 10-12 m €275 €550 €900 8-10 Sail catamaran 40 ft — €420 €1,250 10-12 Sportfisher 10 m — €520 €950 6 Luxury yacht 18-22 m €900 €1,800 €2,800 10-12 Superyacht 25 m+ €1,500 €2,800 €4,500+ 12-20 Pier 7 holds the licence-free boats — see no-license boat rental Marbella for the full rules. Typical departures from Puerto Banús Westbound: head past Estepona, anchor at Cala del Faro for a swim, optional lunch at Sotogrande, return as the sun sets behind Gibraltar. Eastbound: motor along the Golden Mile, swim off Río Verde, push on to Nikki Beach for a tender-in lunch, back via the central Marbella coastline. Sunset 2-hour: a slow westbound cruise to Río Verde and back, perfect for couples — full details on the dedicated sunset cruise Marbella page. How busy does Puerto Banús get? July and August Saturdays: extremely busy. The harbour mouth is single-file traffic with 80–100 boats leaving between 11:00 and 12:00, and the same crowd returning between 17:00 and 19:00. Skippers manage this competently but you'll wait 10–15 minutes at the exit. May, June and September are calmer; weekdays are calm all summer. October through April the marina is half-empty. Booking WhatsApp the date, group size and which fleet category interests you. We send back 2–3 specific boats moored at Puerto Banús with photos, exact pier number and pricing. Same flow works for last-minute weekday bookings (often available same-day in shoulder season). The hub at boat rental Marbella compares Puerto Banús against the other four ports if you're still choosing. Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Andra Kiirkivi Founder & CEO · Boat Rental Marbella "Every booking on this site lands on my phone first. I run the operations end-to-end with our skippers, so the boat you see is the boat that picks you up — same fleet, same crew, no third-party hand-off." If anything goes sideways before, during or after your charter, I'm the person you message. Most replies under 5 minutes during Marbella daytime. Follow @boatrentalmarbella Frequently asked questions Why book from Puerto Banús instead of Marbella Marina? Three reasons: fleet depth, boat size (anything 18 m or above is here by default), and the marina experience. Marbella Marina is more central if you're in the old town. Where exactly do I meet the captain? Each charter sends you a pontoon number and slip 24 hours ahead — typical meeting points are Piers 1 (super-yachts), 2–3 (10–16 m), 6 (sailing/fishing), 7 (day boats and licence-free). Is there parking at Puerto Banús? Yes — underground Aparcamiento Puerto Banús, €2/hour or €18 for the day. Arrive 30 minutes early on summer Saturdays. What boats are available from Puerto Banús? Full Costa del Sol charter inventory: motor yachts 10–25 m, sail/power catamarans 38–55 ft, deep-sea sportfishers, luxury superyachts 24 m+, and a small fleet of licence-free runabouts. Can I sleep on board? Yes on yachts 12 m and above. Overnight charters from Puerto Banús start at €1,200/night for a 4-berth 12 m yacht and rise to €4,000+ for crewed 20 m superyachts. On the water with us Real charters with real groups across Marbella, Puerto Banús and the Costa del Sol. Day charter · group of eight Friends weekend · Marbella coastline Birthday charter · celebrating on the bow Puerto Banús · bow shot ★★★★★ 4.9 · verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/boats/astondoa-40/
TITLE: Astondoa 40 — Astondoa 12.5m Charter from Puerto Banús
DESCRIPTION: Charter the Astondoa 40 from Puerto Banús — 12.5 m, 9 guests, skipper &amp; fuel included. From €749 for 2h.

Home › Boats › Astondoa 40 Astondoa 12.5 m motor yacht · 9 guests · departs Puerto Banús Astondoa 40 — known as "Fufi" — is the Spanish equivalent of the classic Mediterranean motor yacht. Built in Cádiz by Astondoa, fitted out in teak and cream leather, and at home anchoring off the Golden Mile for a long lunch. 12.5 metres, 9 guests, skipper and fuel included. Quick specs: 12.5 m · 9 pax · skipper included · departs Puerto Banús. From €749 for 2h. Drinks, fuel, insurance & VAT included. Hourly pricing Same boat, same crew — price scales with duration. Pick the length that fits your day: Duration Price (EUR) 2h €749 3h €1,049 4h €1,299 5h €1,549 6h €1,799 7h €2,099 8h €2,299 All prices include: licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, vat (21%). No hidden marina fees, no fuel surcharge for the standard coastal route. What makes this boat work Flybridge with shaded bimini and second helm Cream-leather saloon with table-for-six and AC Two private cabins for overnight charter Spanish-built (Astondoa, Cádiz) — local provenance Snorkel gear, inflatable donut & paddleboard on board What's included on every charter Licensed skipper for the full duration Drinks on board: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine and cava Light snacks (fruit, crisps, almonds, biscuits) Fuel for the standard Marbella–Estepona–Cabopino cruising loop Insurance and full safety equipment (life jackets, flares, first aid) Spanish IVA (VAT, 21%) — no surprise at checkout Water toys: snorkel masks, inflatable donut, paddleboard Towels for every guest Catered lunch, premium spirits, DJ and beach-club tender service can be added when you book. Where it departs from The Astondoa 40 berths at Puerto Banús — the deepest charter marina on the Marbella coast and the most central pickup for guests staying on the Golden Mile. You will get the exact pier and slip number 24 hours before departure. Underground parking is 5 minutes from the pontoon. Typical day on the Astondoa 40 Boarding at Puerto Banús — welcome drinks, safety briefing. Westbound run along the Golden Mile, past Marbella Club and Puente Romano. Anchor stop at Cala del Faro or Río Verde for swim, snorkel and paddleboard. Lunch on board (BYO or catered platter). Optional tender to a beach club, or continue east towards Cabopino. Return to Puerto Banús, sunset over La Concha mountain. How to book WhatsApp us with your date and group size. We confirm availability within minutes, hold the boat for 24 hours with no deposit, and lock the booking once you confirm. Full refund up to 7 days out, weather cancellations always 100% refundable. from Puerto Banús, no licence needed From €250 1h skippered View boat → Frequently asked questions How many guests can the Astondoa 40 carry? 9 guests for day charter. Overnight capacity is lower — typically 4–6 in cabins on a 12.5 m yacht. Is the skipper included? Yes — every charter on the Astondoa 40 comes with a licensed Spanish skipper. The captain handles navigation, anchoring and route planning. You and your group are guests for the day. What's included in the price? Skipper, fuel for the standard coastal route, drinks (water and soft drinks), Spanish IVA (VAT) and insurance. Catered lunch and alcohol are extras you can add when booking. Can we go further than the standard route? Yes — longer or further itineraries (Gibraltar day trip, Estepona-Sotogrande loop) are bookable. A fuel surcharge applies for itineraries beyond the standard 12–15 NM coastal cruise. We will quote it before you commit. What happens if the weather is bad? Skipper calls the night before. If forecast wind exceeds Force 4–5 or sea state makes the trip unsafe, you rebook or get a full refund. Light rain alone is not a cancellation reason on the Costa del Sol. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

A great pick for groups who want the upper sun-deck experience. Quick specs: 12.5 m · 11 pax · skipper included · departs Puerto Banús. From €749 for 2h. Drinks, fuel, insurance & VAT included. What makes this boat work Flybridge with second helm and sunbathing pad Sleek Italian styling — Azimut's signature reverse sheer line Large saloon with panoramic windows Two cabins plus convertible saloon for overnight Twin diesel engines, 18-knot cruise What's included on every charter Licensed skipper for the full duration Drinks on board: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine and cava Light snacks (fruit, crisps, almonds, biscuits) Fuel for the standard Marbella–Estepona–Cabopino cruising loop Insurance and full safety equipment (life jackets, flares, first aid) Spanish IVA (VAT, 21%) — no surprise at checkout Water toys: snorkel masks, inflatable donut, paddleboard Towels for every guest Catered lunch, premium spirits, DJ and beach-club tender service can be added when you book. You will get the exact pier and slip number 24 hours before departure. Underground parking is 5 minutes from the pontoon. charter. Overnight capacity is lower — typically 4–6 in cabins on a 12.5 m yacht. Is the skipper included? Yes — every charter on the Azimut 39 comes with a licensed Spanish skipper. The captain handles navigation, anchoring and route planning. You and your group are guests for the day. What's included in the price? Skipper, fuel for the standard coastal route, drinks (water and soft drinks), Spanish IVA (VAT) and insurance. Catered lunch and alcohol are extras you can add when booking. Can we go further than the standard route? Yes — longer or further itineraries (Gibraltar day trip, Estepona-Sotogrande loop) are bookable. If forecast wind exceeds Force 4–5 or sea state makes the trip unsafe, you rebook or get a full refund. No hidden marina fees; fuel is metered and billed separately after the trip. Fuel NOT included on the Dubhe (typically €30–€60 depending on route). All other inclusions per Spanish charter standard. The captain handles navigation, anchoring and route planning. You and your group are guests for the day. What's included in the price? Skipper, drinks (water and soft drinks), Spanish IVA (VAT) and insurance — fuel is metered and billed separately after the trip. Catered lunch and alcohol are extras you can add when booking. Can we go further than the standard route? Yes — longer or further itineraries (Gibraltar day trip, Estepona-Sotogrande loop) are bookable. A fuel surcharge applies for itineraries beyond the standard 12–15 NM coastal cruise. We will quote it before you commit. What happens if the weather is bad? Skipper calls the night before. If forecast wind exceeds Force 4–5 or sea state makes the trip unsafe, you rebook or get a full refund. Light rain alone is not a cancellation reason on the Costa del Sol. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verified reviews From €230 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

TITLE: Fairline Targa 43 — Fairline 14.4m Charter from Puerto Banús
DESCRIPTION: Charter the Fairline Targa 43 from Puerto Banús — 14.4 m, 11 guests, skipper &amp; fuel included. From €1,400 for 4h.

Home › Boats › Fairline Targa 43 Fairline 14.4 m motor yacht · 11 guests · departs Puerto Banús Charter the Fairline Targa 43 — a sleek 14.4 m British sports cruiser built for fast, stylish days on the Costa del Sol. A huge sun-soaked cockpit, bathing platform and sociable layout host up to 11 guests for coastal cruising, swim stops and sundowners. Skippered private day charters from Puerto Banús, Marbella — fuel and captain included. Quick specs: 14.4 m · 11 pax · skipper included · in fleet since 2019 · departs Puerto Banús. From €1,400 for 4h. Drinks, fuel, insurance & VAT included. Hourly pricing Same boat, same crew — price scales with duration. Pick the length that fits your day: Duration Price (EUR) 4h €1,400 6h €1,850 8h €2,350 All prices include: licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, vat (21%). No hidden marina fees, no fuel surcharge for the standard coastal route. What makes this boat work 2 en-suite cabins for overnight charter Swim platform & ladder Paddleboard (SUP) Bluetooth sound system Air conditioning Sun pads What's included on every charter Licensed skipper for the full duration Drinks on board: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine and cava Light snacks (fruit, crisps, almonds, biscuits) Fuel for the standard Marbella–Estepona–Cabopino cruising loop Insurance and full safety equipment (life jackets, flares, first aid) Spanish IVA (VAT, 21%) — no surprise at checkout Water toys: snorkel masks, inflatable donut, paddleboard Towels for every guest Catered lunch, premium spirits, DJ and beach-club tender service can be added when you book. Where it departs from The Fairline Targa 43 berths at Puerto Banús — the deepest charter marina on the Marbella coast and the most central pickup for guests staying on the Golden Mile. You will get the exact pier and slip number 24 hours before departure. Underground parking is 5 minutes from the pontoon. Typical day on the Fairline Targa 43 Boarding at Puerto Banús — welcome drinks, safety briefing. Westbound run along the Golden Mile, past Marbella Club and Puente Romano. Anchor stop at Cala del Faro or Río Verde for swim, snorkel and paddleboard. Lunch on board (BYO or catered platter). Optional tender to a beach club, or continue east towards Cabopino. Return to Puerto Banús, sunset over La Concha mountain. How to book WhatsApp us with your date and group size. We confirm availability within minutes, hold the boat for 24 hours with no deposit, and lock the booking once you confirm. Full refund up to 7 days out, weather cancellations always 100% refundable. €1999 2h skippered View boat → 18.2m · 12 pax Princess V58 Private Princess V58 charter · Puerto Banús, Marbella From €2999 4h skippered View boat → 17.6m · 11 pax Sunseeker Predator 58 Private Sunseeker Predator 58 charter · Puerto Banús, Marbella From €2999 4h skippered View boat → 16m · 12 pax Prestige 500 Private Prestige 500 charter · Puerto Banús, Marbella From €2999 4h skippered View boat → 14.4m · 11 pax Fairline Targa 43 Private Fairline Targa 43 charter · Puerto Banús, Marbella From €1400 4h skippered View boat → 3.4m · 3 pax Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski Sea-Doo GTX & GTI jet skis — self-drive from Puerto Banús, no licence needed From €250 1h skippered View boat → Frequently asked questions How many guests can the Mefasa 90 carry? 12 guests for day charter. Overnight capacity is lower — typically 4–6 in cabins on a 29 m yacht. Is the skipper included? Yes — every charter on the Mefasa 90 comes with a licensed Spanish skipper. The captain handles navigation, anchoring and route planning. You and your group are guests for the day. What's included in the price? Skipper, fuel for the standard coastal route, drinks (water and soft drinks), Spanish IVA (VAT) and insurance. Catered lunch and alcohol are extras you can add when booking. Can we go further than the standard route? Yes — longer or further itineraries (Gibraltar day trip, Estepona-Sotogrande loop) are bookable. A fuel surcharge applies for itineraries beyond the standard 12–15 NM coastal cruise. We will quote it before you commit. What happens if the weather is bad? Skipper calls the night before. If forecast wind exceeds Force 4–5 or sea state makes the trip unsafe, you rebook or get a full refund. Light rain alone is not a cancellation reason on the Costa del Sol. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verified reviews From €7,300 4h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/boats/prestige-500/
TITLE: Prestige 500 — Prestige 16m Charter from Puerto Banús
DESCRIPTION: Charter the Prestige 500 from Puerto Banús — 16 m, 12 guests, skipper &amp; fuel included. From €2,999 for 4h.

Home › Boats › Prestige 500 Prestige 16 m motor yacht · 12 guests · departs Puerto Banús The Prestige 500 is a 16 m flybridge motor yacht designed around space and light — a vast flybridge, full-beam owner's cabin and an easy indoor-outdoor flow. Perfect for relaxed family days, celebrations and sunset cruises for up to 12 guests. Private skippered charters from Marbella, fuel and captain included. Quick specs: 16 m · 12 pax · skipper included · in fleet since 2018 · departs Puerto Banús. From €2,999 for 4h. Drinks, fuel, insurance & VAT included. Hourly pricing Same boat, same crew — price scales with duration. Pick the length that fits your day: Duration Price (EUR) 4h €2,999 6h €4,199 8h €5,799 All prices include: licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, vat (21%). No hidden marina fees, no fuel surcharge for the standard coastal route. What makes this boat work 3 en-suite cabins for overnight charter Flybridge with bar and sunbeds Tender for beach-club drop-offs Swim platform & ladder Paddleboard (SUP) Bluetooth sound system What's included on every charter Licensed skipper for the full duration Drinks on board: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine and cava Light snacks (fruit, crisps, almonds, biscuits) Fuel for the standard Marbella–Estepona–Cabopino cruising loop Insurance and full safety equipment (life jackets, flares, first aid) Spanish IVA (VAT, 21%) — no surprise at checkout Water toys: snorkel masks, inflatable donut, paddleboard Towels for every guest Catered lunch, premium spirits, DJ and beach-club tender service can be added when you book. Where it departs from The Prestige 500 berths at Puerto Banús — the deepest charter marina on the Marbella coast and the most central pickup for guests staying on the Golden Mile. You will get the exact pier and slip number 24 hours before departure. Underground parking is 5 minutes from the pontoon. Typical day on the Prestige 500 Boarding at Puerto Banús — welcome drinks, safety briefing. Westbound run along the Golden Mile, past Marbella Club and Puente Romano. Anchor stop at Cala del Faro or Río Verde for swim, snorkel and paddleboard. Lunch on board (BYO or catered platter). Optional tender to a beach club, or continue east towards Cabopino. Return to Puerto Banús, sunset over La Concha mountain. How to book WhatsApp us with your date and group size. We confirm availability within minutes, hold the boat for 24 hours with no deposit, and lock the booking once you confirm. Full refund up to 7 days out, weather cancellations always 100% refundable. DESCRIPTION: Charter the Princess F70 from Puerto Banús — 21.8 m, 12 guests, skipper &amp; fuel included. From €3,799 for 2h.

Home › Boats › Princess F70 Princess 21.8 m motor yacht · 12 guests · departs Puerto Banús The Princess F70 is a 21.8 m flybridge flagship — a huge flybridge with bar and sunbeds, a full-beam owner's suite and four cabins for overnight comfort. The ultimate platform for celebrations, family days and multi-day cruising for up to 12 guests. Private skippered charters from Marbella. Quick specs: 21.8 m · 12 pax · skipper included · in fleet since 2020 · departs Puerto Banús. From €3,799 for 2h. Drinks, fuel, insurance & VAT included. Hourly pricing Same boat, same crew — price scales with duration. Pick the length that fits your day: Duration Price (EUR) 2h €3,799 4h €6,399 6h €8,899 8h €10,999 All prices include: licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, vat (21%). No hidden marina fees, no fuel surcharge for the standard coastal route. What makes this boat work 4 en-suite cabins for overnight charter Flybridge with bar and sunbeds Tender for beach-club drop-offs Swim platform & ladder Inflatable sea toys Departs Puerto Banús with a licensed skipper, fuel & drinks included What's included on every charter Licensed skipper for the full duration Drinks on board: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine and cava Light snacks (fruit, crisps, almonds, biscuits) Fuel for the standard Marbella–Estepona–Cabopino cruising loop Insurance and full safety equipment (life jackets, flares, first aid) Spanish IVA (VAT, 21%) — no surprise at checkout Water toys: snorkel masks, inflatable donut, paddleboard Towels for every guest Catered lunch, premium spirits, DJ and beach-club tender service can be added when you book. Where it departs from The Princess F70 berths at Puerto Banús — the deepest charter marina on the Marbella coast and the most central pickup for guests staying on the Golden Mile. You will get the exact pier and slip number 24 hours before departure. Underground parking is 5 minutes from the pontoon. DESCRIPTION: Charter the Princess V58 from Puerto Banús — 18.2 m, 12 guests, skipper &amp; fuel included. From €2,999 for 4h.

Home › Boats › Princess V58 Princess 18.2 m motor yacht · 12 guests · departs Puerto Banús The Princess V58 is an 18.2 m British sports yacht blending performance with refined comfort — sweeping sunpads, a spacious cockpit and three elegant cabins below. Perfect for high-style day charters, swim stops and sundowner cruises for up to 12 guests. Skippered, from Marbella / Puerto Banús. Quick specs: 18.2 m · 12 pax · skipper included · in fleet since 2017 · departs Puerto Banús. From €2,999 for 4h. Drinks, fuel, insurance & VAT included. Hourly pricing Same boat, same crew — price scales with duration. URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/boats/sea-doo-gtx-jet-ski/
TITLE: Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski — Sea-Doo (BRP) 3.4m Charter from Puerto Banús
DESCRIPTION: Charter the Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski from Puerto Banús — 3.4 m, 3 guests, skipper &amp; fuel included. From €250 for 1h.

Home › Boats › Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski Sea-Doo (BRP) 3.4 m jet ski · 3 guests · departs Puerto Banús Sea-Doo GTX and GTI jet skis for rent in Marbella, launching from Puerto Banús. Self-drive the Marbella coast on powerful 3-seater Sea-Doos — life jackets and a safety briefing included. No boating licence needed — rental jet skis run under Spain's supervised-rental rules, with a full safety briefing before you set off. €250 for 1 hour, €450 for 2 hours, then €200 each additional hour. Quick specs: 3.4 m · 3 pax · skipper included · in fleet since 2023 · departs Puerto Banús. From €250 for 1h. Drinks, fuel, insurance & VAT included. Hourly pricing Same boat, same crew — price scales with duration. Pick the length that fits your day: Duration Price (EUR) 1h €250 2h €450 3h €650 4h €850 All prices include: licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, vat (21%). No hidden marina fees, no fuel surcharge for the standard coastal route. What makes this boat work Departs Puerto Banús with a licensed skipper, fuel & drinks included What's included on every charter Licensed skipper for the full duration Drinks on board: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine and cava Light snacks (fruit, crisps, almonds, biscuits) Fuel for the standard Marbella–Estepona–Cabopino cruising loop Insurance and full safety equipment (life jackets, flares, first aid) Spanish IVA (VAT, 21%) — no surprise at checkout Water toys: snorkel masks, inflatable donut, paddleboard Towels for every guest Catered lunch, premium spirits, DJ and beach-club tender service can be added when you book. Where it departs from The Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski berths at Puerto Banús — the deepest charter marina on the Marbella coast and the most central pickup for guests staying on the Golden Mile. You will get the exact pier and slip number 24 hours before departure. Underground parking is 5 minutes from the pontoon. Typical day on the Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski Boarding at Puerto Banús — welcome drinks, safety briefing. Westbound run along the Golden Mile, past Marbella Club and Puente Romano. Anchor stop at Cala del Faro or Río Verde for swim, snorkel and paddleboard. Lunch on board (BYO or catered platter). Optional tender to a beach club, or continue east towards Cabopino. Return to Puerto Banús, sunset over La Concha mountain. How to book WhatsApp us with your date and group size. We confirm availability within minutes, hold the boat for 24 hours with no deposit, and lock the booking once you confirm. Full refund up to 7 days out, weather cancellations always 100% refundable. Marbella From €7300 4h skippered View boat → 22.1m · 11 pax Sunseeker Predator 72 Private Sunseeker Predator 72 charter · Puerto Banús, Marbella From €5000 4h skippered View boat → 21.8m · 12 pax €6500 4h skippered View boat → 19m · 10 pax Sunseeker 63 Private Sunseeker 63 charter · Puerto Banús, Marbella From €1999 2h skippered View boat → 18.2m · 12 pax Princess V58 Private Princess V58 charter · Puerto Banús, Marbella From €2999 4h skippered View boat → 17.6m · 11 pax Sunseeker Predator 58 Private Sunseeker Predator 58 charter · Puerto Banús, Marbella From €2999 4h skippered View boat → 16m · 12 pax Prestige 500 Private Prestige 500 charter · Puerto Banús, Marbella From €2999 4h skippered View boat → 14.4m · 11 pax Fairline Targa 43 Private Fairline Targa 43 charter · Puerto Banús, Marbella From €1400 4h skippered View boat → Frequently asked questions How many guests can the Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski carry? 3 guests for day charter. Is the skipper included? Yes — every charter on the Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski comes with a licensed Spanish skipper. The captain handles navigation, anchoring and route planning. You and your group are guests for the day. What's included in the price? Skipper, fuel for the standard coastal route, drinks (water and soft drinks), Spanish IVA (VAT) and insurance. Catered lunch and alcohol are extras you can add when booking. Can we go further than the standard route? Yes — longer or further itineraries (Gibraltar day trip, Estepona-Sotogrande loop) are bookable. A fuel surcharge applies for itineraries beyond the standard 12–15 NM coastal cruise. We will quote it before you commit. What happens if the weather is bad? Skipper calls the night before. If forecast wind exceeds Force 4–5 or sea state makes the trip unsafe, you rebook or get a full refund. Light rain alone is not a cancellation reason on the Costa del Sol. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verified reviews From €250 1h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

Built for show-stopping days on the water, swim breaks in hidden coves and golden-hour cruising for up to 10 guests. Private skippered charters from Marbella. You will get the exact pier and slip number 24 hours before departure. Underground parking is 5 minutes from the pontoon. self-drive from Puerto Banús, no licence needed From €250 1h skippered View boat → Frequently asked questions How many guests can the Sunseeker 63 carry? 10 guests for day charter. Overnight capacity is lower — typically 4–6 in cabins on a 19 m yacht. Is the skipper included? Yes — every charter on the Sunseeker 63 comes with a licensed Spanish skipper. The captain handles navigation, anchoring and route planning. You and your group are guests for the day. What's included in the price? Skipper, fuel for the standard coastal route, drinks (water and soft drinks), Spanish IVA (VAT) and insurance. Catered lunch and alcohol are extras you can add when booking. Can we go further than the standard route? Yes — longer or further itineraries (Gibraltar day trip, Estepona-Sotogrande loop) are bookable. A fuel surcharge applies for itineraries beyond the standard 12–15 NM coastal cruise. We will quote it before you commit. What happens if the weather is bad? Skipper calls the night before. If forecast wind exceeds Force 4–5 or sea state makes the trip unsafe, you rebook or get a full refund. Home › Boats › Sunseeker Predator 58 Sunseeker 17.6 m motor yacht · 11 guests · departs Puerto Banús The Sunseeker Predator 58 is a 17.6 m British performance sport yacht — low, muscular lines, a retractable hardtop and serious pace. Big sunpads, a social cockpit and a swim platform make her a favourite for fast coastal runs and glamorous days at anchor for up to 11 guests. Skippered private charters from Puerto Banús. Based in Puerto Banús, Marbella, with a licensed skipper on every charter. Quick specs: 17.6 m · 11 pax · skipper included · in fleet since 2018 · departs Puerto Banús. From €2,999 for 4h.
