Banús, no licence needed From €250 1h skippered View boat → Frequently asked questions How many guests can the Sunseeker Predator 58 carry? 11 guests for day charter. Overnight capacity is lower — typically 4–6 in cabins on a 17.6 m yacht. Is the skipper included? Yes — every charter on the Sunseeker Predator 58 comes with a licensed Spanish skipper. The captain handles navigation, anchoring and route planning. You and your group are guests for the day. What's included in the price? Skipper, fuel for the standard coastal route, drinks (water and soft drinks), Spanish IVA (VAT) and insurance. Catered lunch and alcohol are extras you can add when booking. Can we go further than the standard route? Yes — longer or further itineraries (Gibraltar day trip, Estepona-Sotogrande loop) are bookable. A fuel surcharge applies for itineraries beyond the standard 12–15 NM coastal cruise. We will quote it before you commit. What happens if the weather is bad? Skipper calls the night before. If forecast wind exceeds Force 4–5 or sea state makes the trip unsafe, you rebook or get a full refund. Light rain alone is not a cancellation reason on the Costa del Sol. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verified reviews From €2,999 4h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

TITLE: Sunseeker Predator 72 — Sunseeker 22.1m Charter from Puerto Banús
DESCRIPTION: Charter the Sunseeker Predator 72 from Puerto Banús — 22.1 m, 11 guests, skipper &amp; fuel included. From €5,000 for 4h.

Home › Boats › Sunseeker Predator 72 Sunseeker 22.1 m motor yacht · 11 guests · departs Puerto Banús The Sunseeker Predator 72 is a 22.1 m thoroughbred sport yacht — striking lines, blistering performance and a sunken beach-club stern. Four cabins, sprawling sun terraces and a glamorous cockpit make her a statement charter for up to 11 guests. Skippered, from Puerto Banús, Marbella. Quick specs: 22.1 m · 11 pax · skipper included · in fleet since 2018 · departs Puerto Banús. From €5,000 for 4h. Drinks, fuel, insurance & VAT included. Hourly pricing Same boat, same crew — price scales with duration. Pick the length that fits your day: Duration Price (EUR) 4h €5,000 6h €6,500 8h €8,000 All prices include: licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, vat (21%). No hidden marina fees, no fuel surcharge for the standard coastal route. What makes this boat work 4 en-suite cabins for overnight charter Sunken beach-club stern for swim-platform days Flybridge with bar and sunbeds Tender for beach-club drop-offs Swim platform & ladder Departs Puerto Banús with a licensed skipper, fuel & drinks included What's included on every charter Licensed skipper for the full duration Drinks on board: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine and cava Light snacks (fruit, crisps, almonds, biscuits) Fuel for the standard Marbella–Estepona–Cabopino cruising loop Insurance and full safety equipment (life jackets, flares, first aid) Spanish IVA (VAT, 21%) — no surprise at checkout Water toys: snorkel masks, inflatable donut, paddleboard Towels for every guest Catered lunch, premium spirits, DJ and beach-club tender service can be added when you book. Where it departs from The Sunseeker Predator 72 berths at Puerto Banús — the deepest charter marina on the Marbella coast and the most central pickup for guests staying on the Golden Mile. You will get the exact pier and slip number 24 hours before departure. Underground parking is 5 minutes from the pontoon. Typical day on the Sunseeker Predator 72 Boarding at Puerto Banús — welcome drinks, safety briefing. Westbound run along the Golden Mile, past Marbella Club and Puente Romano. Anchor stop at Cala del Faro or Río Verde for swim, snorkel and paddleboard. Lunch on board (BYO or catered platter). Optional tender to a beach club, or continue east towards Cabopino. Return to Puerto Banús, sunset over La Concha mountain. How to book WhatsApp us with your date and group size. We confirm availability within minutes, hold the boat for 24 hours with no deposit, and lock the booking once you confirm. Full refund up to 7 days out, weather cancellations always 100% refundable. URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/catamaran-rental-marbella-family/
TITLE: Catamaran Rental Marbella for Families: Spacious and Stable
DESCRIPTION: Book a catamaran rental Marbella family charter for a spacious, stable day on the Costa del Sol. From €749/2h with skipper, drinks &amp; snacks included.

Home › Catamaran Rental Marbella for Families: Spacious and Stable If you are looking for a catamaran rental Marbella family charter that combines space, stability, and all-inclusive ease, the Lagoon 380 is your best option. Unlike monohull motor yachts, catamarans offer a wide, stable platform that does not roll in a swell, making them ideal for young children, elderly parents, or anyone prone to seasickness. Our Lagoon 380 departs from Puerto Banús and can carry up to 10 passengers plus a licensed skipper. Prices start at €749 for two hours and go up to €2,299 for a full eight-hour day – and that includes drinks, snacks, fuel, insurance, and tax. No hidden extras, no surprises. Why a catamaran works for families A catamaran’s twin-hull design gives it a much wider beam than a monohull of the same length. The Lagoon 380 measures 11.6 metres overall but offers a living space that feels more like a 15-metre yacht. The saloon opens directly onto the aft deck, creating a single indoor-outdoor area where parents can keep an eye on children while preparing snacks. Because the hulls are separate, there is no deep V-shaped cabin to trip over – the saloon floor is flat, and the side decks have high guardrails. Children can move around without the constant tilt of a heeling monohull. Our family-friendly catamaran: Lagoon 380 The Lagoon 380 is the only catamaran in our fleet. It is 11.6 metres long, 6.5 metres wide, and can take up to 10 passengers. It departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s most famous marina. The boat has four double cabins and two heads (toilets), so a large family can spread out. The forward trampoline nets are a favourite spot for children to lie on and watch the coastline. The shallow draft of just 1.2 metres means the skipper can anchor very close to the beach at spots like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, where the water is shallow and calm enough for safe swimming. What’s included in the price Every charter on our Lagoon 380 includes a licensed skipper, all fuel, insurance, Spanish IVA (21%), and a complimentary bar with soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. Light snacks are also provided. You do not need to bring anything except sunscreen and towels. The skipper handles navigation, anchoring, and safety briefings, so you can relax. If you want to bring your own food or a birthday cake, the galley has a fridge and a stove. There is no extra charge for mooring at a restaurant dock, though any restaurant bill is yours. Where you can go on a family catamaran trip Departing from Puerto Banús, you have several family-friendly routes. A popular half-day option heads east towards Cabopino, a protected dune reserve with a sandy beach and calm water. The distance is about 5 nautical miles, so the sailing time is short. For a full day, you can go west to Estepona or even Sotogrande, where the coastline opens up with wider bays. The skipper will anchor for swimming and snorkelling at spots like Cala Cortés, a small cove with clear water and rocky seabed. The shallow draft of the catamaran allows it to get closer to the shore than most motor yachts. Pricing and booking Our Lagoon 380 is priced in Tier A: €749 for two hours, €1,199 for four hours, €1,699 for six hours, and €2,299 for eight hours. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and tax. For longer charters or custom requests, simply contact us via WhatsApp. We do not charge extra for cleaning or mooring. To see the full range of boats, visit our Marbella boat rental hub. Safety and comfort for all ages The Lagoon 380 has high bulwarks and guardrails all around, reducing the risk of falls. The saloon door can be latched open or closed, and the galley is equipped with a gas cooker and a fridge. Lifejackets for children and adults are on board, and the skipper will give a short safety briefing before departure. The boat’s stability means that even on a breezy day, you will not feel the constant rolling that can upset younger children. Many families choose a four-hour morning charter (10:00–14:00) when the sea is calmest and the sun is not too strong. Frequently asked questions Is the Lagoon 380 suitable for young children? Yes. The Lagoon 380’s wide beam and low freeboard make it very stable, so toddlers and older kids can move around safely. The skipper will anchor in calm, shallow bays like Cala Cortés where children can swim under supervision. What is included in the catamaran rental price? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, Spanish IVA (21%), and an open bar of soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, plus light snacks. No extra charges for mooring or cleaning. How many people can the Lagoon 380 carry? The Lagoon 380 has a maximum capacity of 10 passengers plus the skipper. This makes it ideal for a family group or two families sharing the day. Do we need to bring our own food and drinks? No. Soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks are provided. If you want a specific meal or cake for a celebration, you can bring your own – there is a fridge and galley on board. Can we swim and snorkel during the trip? Absolutely. The skipper will anchor at protected spots like Cala del Faro or Cabopino, where the water is calm and clear. Snorkelling gear is usually available on request. What happens in bad weather? Your safety is the priority. If the forecast shows strong winds or high seas, we will offer you a free reschedule or full refund. The skipper monitors conditions and will adjust the route. How do we book a catamaran for our family? Contact us via WhatsApp on the number shown on our website. We will confirm availability for the Lagoon 380, send you a booking link, and answer any specific requests you have. Ready to book your catamaran rental Marbella family day out? The Lagoon 380 offers the space, stability, and all-inclusive value that families need. For other options, check our full fleet of boats in Marbella or ask us directly on WhatsApp. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/catamaran-rental-marbella/
TITLE: Catamaran Rental Marbella 2026: Sail &amp; Power Catamarans from €350
DESCRIPTION: Looking for catamaran rental in Marbella? Our current fleet is two 12.5 m flybridge motor yachts from Puerto Banús (€749/2h). Plus a full guide to catamaran charter options in Marbella.

Home › Catamaran Rental Marbella 2026: Sail & Power Catamarans from €350 Our fleet right now: two 12.5 m flybridge motor yachts — the Astondoa 40 (9 guests) and Azimut 39 (11 guests). From €749 for 2h , up to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day . Skipper, fuel, drinks, insurance and VAT included. Catamaran rental in Marbella means a stable twin-hull platform, far more deck space than a monohull yacht of the same length, and a flat-water ride that keeps even seasickness-prone guests relaxed. Prices start at €350 for 4 hours on a 38-ft sail catamaran from Puerto Banús and scale up to €2,400 for a full day on a 50-ft luxury catamaran. Why a catamaran beats a monohull in Marbella Three concrete advantages for the typical Marbella day charter: Stability. The Costa del Sol gets a 12–18 knot easterly (the levante) most summer afternoons. A monohull yacht heels 15–20°; a catamaran heels 2–3°. Glasses stay on tables, kids stay calm, and lunch stays on the plate. Deck space. A 40 ft catamaran has the deck area of roughly a 55 ft monohull. Bridgedeck cockpit, two helm stations, forward trampolines for sun-lounging, and a swim platform across the full stern. Shallow draft. Catamarans typically draw 1.1–1.3 metres versus 2.0–2.5 metres for a monohull. The skipper can anchor closer to shore at Cala del Faro, Cabopino dunes or Río Real — which puts the swim ladder over clearer, calmer water. Catamaran rental Marbella prices 2026 Catamaran 4 h low 4 h high Full day 8 h Day guests 38–42 ft sail catamaran €350 €420 €1,000–€1,250 10–12 44–48 ft sail catamaran €450 €580 €1,400–€1,700 12 40–45 ft power catamaran €520 €680 €1,600–€1,900 10–12 50 ft luxury catamaran €700 €900 €1,800–€2,400 12–14 Prices include skipper, fuel and standard drinks. For comparison with motor yachts of similar length, see yacht charter Marbella . Sail vs power catamaran Sail catamarans are the romantic, atmospheric option. When the wind cooperates (most afternoons May–October), the skipper unfurls the genoa and you sail in near silence at 6–8 knots. The downside: under engine alone, sail catamarans only do 7–9 knots, so reaching Sotogrande and back in 4 hours isn't realistic. Power catamarans cruise at 18–22 knots with twin diesels, which makes long itineraries viable, but you trade the quiet sail experience for engine noise. For pure swim-and-lunch days where range doesn't matter, the sail catamaran is almost always the better experience. For Gibraltar trips or anywhere you want to cover ground, the power cat or a motor yacht makes more sense. Catamaran itineraries from Puerto Banús The 4-hour catamaran sweet spot from Puerto Banús: depart 11:00, motor west 30 minutes to clear the marina traffic, raise sails off Río Verde, sail the Golden Mile coastline downwind, drop sails and anchor at Río Real for a long swim, motor back to Puerto Banús by 15:00 in time for a beach club afternoon. For a full day, add a second anchor stop at Cala del Faro past Estepona — total round trip about 35 NM. If you have kids on board , the flat catamaran deck is dramatically safer than a heeling monohull. What's included Standard catamaran charter inclusions in Marbella are identical to motor yacht charters: skipper, fuel, soft drinks, water, ice, light snacks, snorkel gear and an inflatable SUP or donut. Many catamaran operators also throw in fishing rods (light tackle for tuna or mackerel) and the use of the on-board BBQ at anchor — ask when booking. Best months for catamaran rental in Marbella May, June, September and October are ideal — steady 8–14 knot wind for proper sailing, sea temperature 19–23 °C, and 10–15% lower prices than peak July/August. July and August deliver hotter weather but variable wind: sometimes glassy mornings (motor only) and stronger afternoons. November to March, sail catamaran trips still run on calm days but choice of operators is more limited. How to book Tell us your date, group size, sail vs power preference and budget. We'll send back specific catamaran options with photos and exact pricing. Confirm with 50% deposit; balance on the boarding day. The full boat rental Marbella hub compares catamarans against every other boat type if you're still deciding. Frequently asked questions Why pick a catamaran over a monohull yacht? Two reasons: stability and deck space. A catamaran sits flat on the water with two hulls — even in 15-knot wind there's almost no heel — so guests who get queasy on monohulls can usually relax. The bridgedeck and forward trampolines also give you 40–50% more usable deck space at the same overall length, which matters when you have 10+ guests. How much is catamaran rental in Marbella? Catamaran rental in Marbella starts at €350 for 4 hours on a 38-ft sail catamaran in low season, €420 in high season. An 8-hour day on the same catamaran is €1,000–€1,250 fully crewed. Larger 50-ft catamarans run €600 for 4 hours and €1,800–€2,400 for a day. Sail catamaran or power catamaran? Sail catamarans give you the option to actually sail when the wind is right (typically a Levante easterly mid-afternoon) — it's quieter and more romantic, but slower. Power catamarans (twin-engine, no rig) deliver 18–22 knot cruising, which matters if you want to reach Sotogrande or Cabopino on the same day. For lunch-and-swim half-days, sail catamarans win on atmosphere. How many guests fit on a catamaran? Day-charter capacity on the standard 38–42 ft catamarans in Marbella is 10–12 guests. Larger 50-ft catamarans take 14–16 day guests. Overnight capacity is lower — typically 6–8 in cabins. For groups above 16, look at boat party options on motor yachts. Are catamarans good for snorkelling? Excellent. The shallow draft (often 1.2 m vs 2.5 m for a monohull) lets the skipper anchor closer to shore, which usually means clearer water for snorkelling. The wide swim platforms at the stern make getting in and out far easier than climbing down a monohull ladder. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

The Costa del Sol's calm Mediterranean waters make it perfect for onboard meetings, team-building activities, or sunset cruises. Our boats are equipped with comfortable saloons, sun decks, and sound systems, allowing you to tailor the experience to your corporate goals. Plus, with a licensed skipper included, there's no need for a boating license. Our Fleet for Corporate Events We offer a diverse range of vessels to suit different group sizes and budgets. A typical 4-hour trip might include a stop at a secluded cove for swimming, followed by a relaxed cruise back with drinks. For a full-day event (8 hours), we recommend heading to the dunes of Cabopino or the chic port of Sotogrande. How to Book Booking is straightforward. Contact us on WhatsApp with your preferred date, number of guests, and budget. We'll recommend the best boat from our fleet and confirm availability. Secure payment is processed, and you'll receive a confirmation with departure details. For last-minute requests, we can often accommodate within 48 hours. Frequently asked questions What is the best boat for a corporate event in Marbella? For team days, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers a spacious flybridge and saloon. For client entertainment, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) includes a jet ski and premium amenities. Both depart from Puerto Banús. How much does corporate boat hire cost in Marbella? URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/day-charter-marbella/
TITLE: Day Charter Marbella: All-Inclusive Yacht Hire
DESCRIPTION: Book an all-inclusive day charter Marbella from €749/2h. Licensed skipper, drinks &amp; snacks included. Depart Puerto Banús. Instant WhatsApp booking.

Home › Day Charter Marbella: All-Inclusive Yacht Hire For a day charter Marbella that combines luxury, convenience, and transparent pricing, our fleet departs from Puerto Banús with everything included. No hidden fees, no extra charges for skipper or fuel – just you, the Mediterranean, and a choice of yachts from €749 for 2 hours up to 12 passengers. Why choose an all-inclusive day charter Marbella? Every booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). That means the price you see is the price you pay. Whether you take a 2-hour spin along the Golden Mile or an 8-hour cruise to Estepona, there are no surprises. Our base is Puerto Banús, the heart of Marbella's yachting scene. From here, you can reach Cabopino in 20 minutes, Cala del Faro in 30, or Estepona in 45. All routes are flexible – your skipper adapts to your plans. Day charter Marbella pricing – clear and competitive Yacht Length Max guests Price from Astondoa 40 12.5m 9 €749 / 2h → €2,299 / 8h Azimut 39 12.5m 11 €749 / 2h → €2,299 / 8h Mangusta 80 24m 12 €4,719 / 4h (jet ski included) Other yachts 8m–29m 5–12 Price on request via WhatsApp For the Mangusta 80, the minimum charter is 4 hours and a free jet ski is included. All other yachts are priced individually – just message us on WhatsApp for a custom quote. Our fleet for your day charter Marbella We operate 27 boats from Puerto Banús. Here are the most popular for day charters: Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) – ideal for small groups, great fuel economy, sunbathing aft deck. Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – sporty flybridge, extra seating, perfect for families. Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) – open sport yacht, top speed 38 knots, jet ski included. Sunseeker Predator 58 (17.6m, 11 pax) – iconic British design, spacious cockpit. Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) – flybridge luxury, full galley, stabilisers for comfort. For a complete list, visit our fleet overview . Popular itineraries for your day charter Marbella Your skipper knows the coast. Suggest one of these routes: Puerto Banús → Cabopino (20 min each way) – swim at Cabopino beach, lunch at a chiringuito, return. Puerto Banús → Cala del Faro (30 min each way) – secluded cove with clear water, snorkelling spot. Puerto Banús → Estepona (45 min each way) – harbour town with marina restaurants, dolphin spotting en route. Puerto Banús → Sotogrande (1 hour each way) – exclusive resort, golf course views, longer cruise. All routes can be mixed. Want to anchor off the Golden Mile and watch the sunset? Just say so. What to expect on a day charter Marbella On arrival at Puerto Banús, your skipper will greet you at the dock. After a safety briefing, you cast off. The boat has a shaded saloon, sun pads, a bathroom, and a sound system. Drinks and snacks are served throughout. You can swim, sunbathe, or just enjoy the views of La Concha mountain. Most charters include a stop for swimming. If you book the Mangusta 80, the jet ski adds a thrill. The skipper will also point out landmarks like the Río Verde beach and the luxury villas of the Golden Mile. Book your day charter Marbella today Booking is simple. Choose your yacht, send us a WhatsApp message, and we confirm availability within minutes. No online booking fees, no deposit stress. Payment is made before departure. For a day charter Marbella that delivers on value and experience, browse our full fleet and message us now. Frequently asked questions What is included in a day charter Marbella? Every day charter Marbella includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs. How much does a day charter Marbella cost? Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39. The Mangusta 80 costs €4,719 for a minimum 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. Other yachts are priced on request via WhatsApp. Do I need a boat licence to charter a yacht? No. Every day charter Marbella comes with a fully licensed skipper who handles navigation, mooring, and safety. You just relax. Can I bring my own food and drinks? Yes. You may bring additional food and drinks. Our standard charter includes a selection of beverages and snacks, but you are welcome to customise. How many people can join a day charter? Capacity varies by yacht. The Dubhe (8m) takes up to 5 passengers, the Astondoa 40 takes 9, and most larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 or Ferretti 94 take 12 passengers. Where do day charters depart from? All our day charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. We can arrange pick-up from Marbella Marina or other nearby ports on request. What should I wear on a day charter? Light clothing, swimwear, sunscreen, a hat, and non-slip shoes. Even in summer, bring a light jacket or windbreaker for the afternoon breeze. Can I cancel or reschedule my day charter? Yes, subject to our cancellation policy. Contact us via WhatsApp at least 48 hours before departure for a full refund or reschedule. Weather cancellations are free. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises

Bootsverleih › Ohne Führerschein In Spanien kannst du ein Boot ohne Lizenz mieten, solange folgende Regeln eingehalten werden: maximal 5 m Rumpflänge, maximal 15 PS Motor, innerhalb 2 Seemeilen von der Küste, nur bei Tageslicht, Kapitän mindestens 18 Jahre alt . Auf dieser Basis bieten wir mehrere führerscheinfreie Boote ab Puerto Banús — billigstes ab €230 für 2 Stunden. URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/de/bootsverleih-puerto-banus/
TITLE: Bootsverleih Puerto Banús: Marbella&#x27;s Top-Marina für Yachten
DESCRIPTION: Bootsverleih Puerto Banús — der wichtigste Yachthafen Marbellas. Ab €749/2h für skipperte Charters. Tiefe Liegeplätze, Luxusboutiquen-Promenade.

Bootsverleih › Puerto Banús Puerto Banús ist der primäre Hafen für Yachtcharter in Marbella — der größte Marina an der Costa del Sol, mit den tiefsten Liegeplätzen, der berühmten Boutique-Promenade und direktem Zugang zur Goldenen Meile. Fast jede Yacht über 12 m startet von hier, einschließlich unserer gesamten Hauptflotte. Was Puerto Banús besonders macht Tiefliegeplätze — Yachten bis 80 m liegen hier. Astondoa 40, Azimut 39 und Mangusta 80 sind alle hier stationiert. Luxus-Promenade — Louis Vuitton, Cartier, Hermès, Sea Grill, Casanis, La Sala — alles 2 Minuten zu Fuß vom Pontoon. Parken — der Hafen hat sein eigenes Parkhaus, €1.50/Stunde, immer Plätze verfügbar. Nightlife — Olivia Valere, La Sala Banús, Pangea sind alle 5 Minuten vom Hafen. Wie du Puerto Banús erreichst Vom Flughafen Málaga (AGP): 50 Minuten mit Taxi/Uber (~€85) oder Mietwagen über die AP-7. Vom Flughafen Gibraltar: 45 Minuten. Vom Bahnhof Marbella zur Marina: 12 Minuten mit Taxi. Yacht-Pickup-Punkt Wir treffen euch am Eingang zum Hafen-Bürozentrum (Marina Office), 5 Minuten vor der gebuchten Charter-Zeit. Andra schickt euch den genauen Pin per WhatsApp 24 Stunden vor dem Charter. Andere Häfen Wenn Puerto Banús zu hektisch ist, bieten wir auch Pickup in Marbella Marina (zentral, ruhiger), Cabopino (östlich, dünenfreundlich) oder Sotogrande (westlich, für Polo-Liebhaber). Schreib auf WhatsApp, wenn du einen anderen Pickup brauchst. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verifizierte Bewertungen Ab €749 2h Charter mit Skipper WhatsApp jetzt — durchschnittliche Antwort in unter 5 Minuten. URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/de/katamaran-mieten-marbella/
TITLE: Katamaran mieten Marbella 2026: stabil, geräumig, familienfreundlich
DESCRIPTION: Katamaran mieten in Marbella — Doppelrumpf-Stabilität, große Sonnenflächen, ideal für Familien &amp; Gruppen. Ab €749/2h mit Skipper, Getränken &amp; MwSt. ab Puerto Banús.

Bootsverleih › Katamaran mieten Ein Katamaran ist die ruhigste und stabilste Art, die Küste von Marbella zu erleben. Durch die zwei Rümpfe schaukelt er kaum — perfekt für Familien mit Kindern, Großeltern oder alle, die seekrankheitsempfindlich sind. Dazu kommt viel mehr Deckfläche als auf einer gleich langen Motoryacht: ein großes Trampolin am Bug, ein schattiger Cockpit-Bereich und breite Sonnenliegen. Unser Lagoon 380 startet ab Puerto Banús. Warum ein Katamaran in Marbella? Stabilität — fast kein Rollen, ideal gegen Seekrankheit. Platz — Trampolin-Netze am Bug zum Sonnen, separater Schattenbereich. Flacher Tiefgang — kommt näher an Strände und kleine Buchten (Calas) als eine tiefkielige Yacht. Familienfreundlich — Kinder können sich sicher bewegen, viel Platz für Schwimmpausen. Preise Dauer Preis Ideal für 2 Stunden €749 Schnuppertour + ein Schwimmstopp 4 Stunden €1.299 Halbtag mit Mittagessen an Bord 6 Stunden €1.799 Mehrere Buchten, Paddleboard, Schnorcheln 8 Stunden €2.299 Ganzer Tag — bis Sotogrande oder Cabopino Skipper, Treibstoff, Getränke, Schnorchelausrüstung und 21% MwSt. sind inklusive. Größere Gruppen sehen sich die Familien-Yachtcharter oder die Luxusyachten an. Häufige Fragen Ist ein Katamaran gut gegen Seekrankheit? Ja — die zwei Rümpfe machen ihn deutlich stabiler als ein Einrumpfboot. Wer schnell seekrank wird, ist hier am besten aufgehoben. Wie viele Personen passen drauf? Komfortabel für Familien und Gruppen bis ~10 Personen tagsüber. Schreib uns die genaue Gruppengröße auf WhatsApp und wir empfehlen das passende Boot. Wo startet der Katamaran? Ab Puerto Banús . Pickup in Marbella Marina, Cabopino oder Estepona auf Anfrage möglich. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verifizierte Bewertungen Ab €749 2h Charter mit Skipper WhatsApp jetzt — durchschnittliche Antwort in unter 5 Minuten. Canados 86 & Ferretti 94 — auf Anfrage für den ganz großen Auftritt. TITLE: Sonnenuntergang-Bootstour Marbella: Sunset Cruise ab €749
DESCRIPTION: Sonnenuntergang-Bootstour in Marbella — Cava an Deck, goldenes Licht über der Goldenen Meile, Skipper inklusive. Ab €749/2h ab Puerto Banús.

Bootsverleih › Sonnenuntergang-Tour Der späte Nachmittag ist die magischste Zeit, um vor Marbella aufs Wasser zu gehen: Der Wind legt sich, die See wird glatt, und die Sonne taucht den Berg La Concha und die Skyline von Puerto Banús in goldenes Licht. Unsere Sonnenuntergang-Touren starten so, dass ihr genau zum Sonnenuntergang mit einem Glas Cava in der Hand draußen seid. Was eine Sunset-Tour besonders macht Cava & Getränke an Deck — angestoßen wird, wenn die Sonne den Horizont berührt. Ruhigste See des Tages — abends ist das Wasser meist spiegelglatt. Bestes Licht für Fotos — perfekt auch für private Anlässe , Heiratsanträge oder Jubiläen. Schwimmstopp möglich — bei warmem Wasser ein Bad im Abendlicht. Zeiten & Preis Die ideale 2-Stunden-Tour startet je nach Jahreszeit zwischen 18:30 und 20:30 Uhr. Preis ab €749 für 2 Stunden auf einer 12,5 m Yacht ( Astondoa 40 oder Azimut 39 ) — Skipper, Treibstoff, Cava, Getränke und MwSt. inklusive. Für einen feierlichen Anlass empfehlen sich auch die Luxusyachten . Häufige Fragen Wann genau geht die Sonne unter? Im Hochsommer gegen 21:30 Uhr, im Frühling/Herbst gegen 19:00–20:00 Uhr. Wir stimmen die Startzeit auf den exakten Sonnenuntergang ab — sag uns einfach dein Datum. Ist das romantisch genug für einen Heiratsantrag? Sehr — schreib uns vorab auf WhatsApp , wir kümmern uns diskret um Extras wie Blumen oder eine Torte. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verifizierte Bewertungen Ab €749 2h Charter mit Skipper WhatsApp jetzt — durchschnittliche Antwort in unter 5 Minuten. Hier liegen unsere Yachten an tiefen Liegeplätzen, nur wenige Schritte von der berühmten Boutique-Promenade entfernt. Vom Pontoon bis zur offenen See sind es nur Minuten. Yachten ab Puerto Banús Astondoa 40 'Fufi' & Azimut 39 — 12,5 m, ab €749/2h. Mangusta 80 'Nina' — 24 m Luxus-Flaggschiff ab €4.719/4h. Weitere Yachten und Katamarane auf Anfrage. URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/es/alquiler-barcos-puerto-banus/
TITLE: Alquiler de barcos en Puerto Banús 2026 — desde €749/2h
DESCRIPTION: Alquiler de barcos en Puerto Banús desde €749 para 2 horas. Nuestros yates a motor de 12,5 m (Astondoa 40 y Azimut 39) — patrón, bebidas y combustible incluidos.

Inicio › Alquiler de barcos en Puerto Banús Puerto Banús es la marina más emblemática de la Costa del Sol y la base de nuestra flota. Salimos desde aquí cada día con yates a motor de 12,5 m (Astondoa 40 y Azimut 39) y el yate de lujo Mangusta 80 — desde €749 por 2 horas , patrón, bebidas, combustible y todo incluido. Por qué salir desde Puerto Banús Tres motivos prácticos: Profundidad y tamaño. Puerto Banús tiene 915 amarres y la bocana más profunda de la zona — único puerto local capaz de acoger yates de más de 30 metros como nuestro Mangusta 80. Concentración de flota. Aquí amarran el 60% de los charters disponibles entre Estepona y Cabopino. Todos nuestros barcos están aquí. Infraestructura. Repostaje, agua, electricidad, chandlers, restaurantes a 5 minutos del pantalán — todo Marbella charter en un solo punto. Dónde encontrar el barco Puerto Banús se organiza en 8 pantalanes paralelos numerados del 1 al 8 desde oeste a este. Nuestros yates atracan habitualmente en: Pantalán 1 — Mangusta 80 (superyate) Pantalanes 2-3 — Astondoa 40 y Azimut 39 (yates de 12,5 m) Te enviamos el pantalán y amarre exacto 24 horas antes. Llega 15 minutos antes — los charters mediterráneos salen puntuales. Aparcamiento y logística Aparcamiento Puerto Banús (subterráneo, 1.200 plazas) — €2/hora, €18/día. Se llena los sábados de verano a partir de las 11:00, así que llega 30 minutos antes. El aparcamiento gratuito en la calle al oeste de la marina nunca funciona en julio-agosto. Comida antes de embarcar: Antonio's Beach Club (tapas), Picasso (cocina española, servicio rápido), o un bocadillo del SuperSol en Calle Ribera. Precios por hora desde Puerto Banús Duración Astondoa 40 / Azimut 39 2 horas €749 4 horas €1.299 6 horas €1.799 8 horas €2.299 Mangusta 80 (mínimo 4h): desde €4.719. Ver página del barco . Itinerarios típicos desde Puerto Banús Oeste: Estepona, fondeo en Cala del Faro para baño, regreso al atardecer. Este: Milla de Oro, baño en Río Verde, parada en Cabopino para comida. Atardecer 2h: Lento por la Milla de Oro, fondeo breve frente a Nikki Beach, regreso con el sol cayendo tras La Concha. Mejor época del año para salir desde Puerto Banús Mayo, junio y septiembre son los meses ideales — aguas cálidas (21-23°C), viento moderado por las mañanas y precios estables. Julio y agosto son pico de demanda con calor máximo (sea 24°C) pero también vientos de levante por las tardes que pueden hacer la mar más movida. Reserva con 2-3 semanas de antelación para sábados de julio y agosto. Octubre sigue ofreciendo buen tiempo con menos turistas — una de las mejores apuestas para grupos. ¿Qué hace Puerto Banús diferente al resto de marinas? Tres ventajas concretas: Profundidad y tamaño: 915 amarres y la bocana más profunda del litoral — único puerto local capaz de acoger superyates como nuestro Mangusta 80 de 24 m. Servicios: repostaje, agua, electricidad y restaurantes a 5 minutos de los pantalanes. Antonio's Beach Club, Picasso y Mosh para comer antes de embarcar. Vida nocturna: la marina conecta directamente con la zona de bares y discotecas, perfecto si quieres alargar la noche después del charter (Bocaccio a 90 segundos del pantalán). Si prefieres una marina más tranquila y central, ver la comparación Puerto Banús vs Marbella Marina (en inglés). Preguntas frecuentes ¿Cuánto cuesta alquilar un barco en Puerto Banús? Desde €749 por 2 horas en nuestro Astondoa 40 o Azimut 39, hasta €2.299 por un día completo. Todo incluido: patrón, combustible, bebidas, IVA. ¿Dónde aparco? Aparcamiento subterráneo Puerto Banús (€2/hora, €18/día). Llega 30 minutos antes en verano. ¿Se puede dormir a bordo? Sí en barcos de 12 m o más. Charter nocturno desde €1.200, se cotiza por WhatsApp. Con nuestros clientes a bordo Charters reales con grupos reales en Marbella, Puerto Banús y la Costa del Sol. URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/es/alquiler-barcos-sin-licencia-marbella/
TITLE: Alquiler de barcos sin licencia en Marbella — con patrón desde €749
DESCRIPTION: Alquiler de barcos sin licencia en Marbella: nuestra flota va con patrón profesional, así que tú no necesitas titulación. Desde €749/2h con todo incluido.

Inicio › Alquiler de barcos sin licencia en Marbella El truco más rápido: no necesitas licencia náutica para alquilar un barco en Marbella si reservas uno de nuestros yates con patrón profesional incluido. Desde €749 por 2 horas , todo cubierto: patrón, combustible, bebidas, seguro, IVA. Las dos opciones reales en Marbella 1. Reserva con patrón (lo que hacemos nosotros) El patrón conduce el barco. Tú y tus invitados sois pasajeros, así que no necesitas licencia náutica . Sólo necesitas DNI o pasaporte. Desde €749/2h en nuestra flota ( Astondoa 40 o Azimut 39 ) — el 95% de los alquileres en Marbella funcionan así. 2. Pequeña embarcación sin titulación (5 m / 15 cv) La ley española permite conducir embarcaciones de hasta 5 metros con motor de hasta 15 cv sin licencia, dentro de 2 millas náuticas de la costa, en horario diurno y con conductor mayor de 18 años. Hay operadores que alquilan estos barcos en Cabopino, pero nosotros no operamos esta categoría — preferimos garantizar la experiencia con patrón. Por qué pagar el patrón vale la pena Cero papeleo: sólo subes a bordo y disfrutas. Conoce la costa: nuestros patrones saben dónde está el agua más calmada, los mejores fondeos, los chiringuitos donde se puede atracar. Maniobras complicadas: entrar a Puerto Banús un sábado de agosto no es trivial. El patrón lo resuelve. Más diversión, menos estrés: tú con cava en la mano, no con el manual del barco. Reglas de embarcaciones sin licencia en España Si prefieres conducir tú mismo, las reglas estatales son: Eslora máxima del casco: 5 metros (motor) o 6 m (vela) Potencia máxima del motor: 15 caballos Distancia máxima desde costa: 2 millas náuticas Sólo horario diurno (hasta la puesta de sol) Conductor con 18 años cumplidos y DNI/pasaporte Sin sobrepasar el límite de alcohol en sangre del conducir Qué incluye nuestro alquiler con patrón Patrón con titulación PER (Patrón de Embarcaciones de Recreo) o superior Combustible para la ruta costera estándar Bebidas: agua, refrescos, cerveza, vino blanco y cava Snacks ligeros Snorkel, donut hinchable, paddleboard, toallas Seguro completo a terceros y daños propios IVA español (21%) — sin sorpresas en el muelle Precios Duración Yate con patrón 2 horas €749 4 horas €1.299 6 horas €1.799 8 horas €2.299 Qué incluye un día con patrón frente a alquilar sin licencia Aspecto Con patrón (nuestra flota) Sin licencia (5 m / 15 cv) Capacidad 9–12 invitados 4–5 personas Distancia de la costa Sin límite práctico Máximo 2 millas náuticas Horario Día completo o noche Solo de día (antes del ocaso) Bebidas a bordo Cava, vino blanco, cerveza, refrescos Lo que lleves tú Comodidades Aire acondicionado, baño, cocina Bañera básica, sin baño Conducción Patrón profesional Tú conduces Precio 2 h €749 total €130–€180 total Precio por persona (grupo de 6) ~€125 por persona ~€25 por persona si grupo de 5 Para grupos pequeños y presupuesto ajustado, una embarcación sin licencia tiene sentido. Para cualquier cosa con grupo de 6+, comodidad, bebidas a bordo o llegar a sitios bonitos como Cala del Faro, nuestra opción con patrón sale a cuenta y es infinitamente más relajante. Sobre los patrones de nuestra flota Todos nuestros patrones tienen titulación oficial española (PER — Patrón de Embarcaciones de Recreo, o superior) y al menos 5 años de experiencia en la Costa del Sol. Conocen cada fondeo, cada chiringuito accesible por mar, las horas óptimas para evitar el viento de levante, y cómo maniobrar Puerto Banús un sábado de agosto. Hablan español, inglés y la mayoría también francés o italiano. Dejarán que conduzcas un rato en tramos seguros si te apetece probar el timón. Reglamentación española al detalle La normativa de embarcaciones sin titulación viene del Real Decreto 875/2014. Los límites simultáneos son: Eslora máxima: 5 m (motor) / 6 m (vela) Potencia máxima: 15 cv Distancia máxima de la costa: 2 millas náuticas (~3,7 km) Solo horario diurno, sin pernoctación Conductor con al menos 18 años cumplidos Misma normativa de alcohol que para conducción terrestre El Servicio Marítimo de la Guardia Civil hace patrullas aleatorias en julio-agosto, especialmente entre Puerto Banús y Cabopino. Si te paran y el motor supera los 15 cv en una embarcación supuestamente "sin titulación", la multa al alquilador es de €600+ y al conductor le retiran el timón en el momento. Por eso los operadores serios son estrictos con el límite. Preguntas frecuentes ¿De verdad puedo alquilar un barco sin licencia en Marbella? Sí — con dos opciones. Reserva un yate con patrón profesional (nuestra flota, desde €749/2h) y eres pasajero, sin ningún requisito de licencia. O alquila una embarcación pequeña (hasta 5 m / 15 cv) en operadores especializados, sin titulación pero con limitaciones (2 NM de costa, sólo de día). ¿Hace falta saber navegar? No con nuestros barcos — el patrón conduce. Sólo te sientas, disfrutas y das instrucciones del tipo "un baño más antes de volver". ¿Cuántos puedo llevar? Astondoa 40 hasta 9 invitados, Azimut 39 hasta 11, Mangusta 80 hasta 12. ¿Puedo conducir un rato? Sí, durante tramos seguros y bajo supervisión del patrón. Es una práctica habitual y los patrones suelen estar encantados de que pruebes el timón. Con nuestros clientes a bordo Charters reales con grupos reales en Marbella, Puerto Banús y la Costa del Sol. TITLE: Alquiler de yates en Marbella 2026: Yates a motor con patrón desde €749
DESCRIPTION: Alquiler de yates en Marbella desde €749/2h en nuestros yates a motor de 12,5 m. Patrón, combustible, bebidas y IVA incluidos. Salida desde Puerto Banús — reserva por WhatsApp.

Inicio › Alquiler de yates en Marbella El alquiler de yates en Marbella significa subir a uno de nuestros yates a motor de 12,5 metros con flybridge, conducido por un patrón profesional desde Puerto Banús. Patrón, combustible, bebidas (cerveza, vino blanco, cava), seguro e IVA incluidos. Desde €749 por 2 horas. Nuestros yates a motor Azimut 39 — yate italiano con flybridge, 12,5 m, hasta 11 invitados. Líneas modernas, dos cubiertas, segundo puesto de mando arriba. Astondoa 40 — yate español clásico, 12,5 m, hasta 9 invitados. Interior en teca y crema. El "Fufi" del muelle de Puerto Banús. Mangusta 80 — la nave insignia de la flota. 24 m italianos con moto de agua incluida y patrón + tripulación. Desde €4.719 por 4 horas. Charter de lujo. Precios por hora Duración Astondoa 40 / Azimut 39 Mangusta 80 (mínimo 4h) 2 h €749 — 4 h €1.299 €4.719 6 h €1.799 €6.500+ 8 h €2.299 €9.000+ Todos los precios incluyen patrón, combustible, bebidas, snacks, seguro e IVA. Para el detalle del Mangusta 80 (24 m con moto de agua), consulta su página específica . Itinerario típico de medio día 11:00 Embarque en Puerto Banús, bebida de bienvenida. 11:30 Salimos rumbo oeste por la Milla de Oro. 12:30 Fondeamos en Cala del Faro o Río Verde para baño y snorkel. 14:00 Comida a bordo (BYO o catering opcional). 15:00 Regreso a Puerto Banús con vistas a La Concha. Qué incluye Patrón con titulación PER o superior Combustible para la ruta costera estándar Bebidas: agua, refrescos, cerveza, vino blanco y cava Snacks ligeros Snorkel, donut hinchable, paddleboard Toallas para cada invitado Seguro a terceros y daños propios IVA español 21% Cómo reservar Mándanos un WhatsApp con fecha, número de invitados y preferencia de barco. Respondemos en menos de 5 minutos con cotización exacta y disponibilidad. Sin coste hasta que confirmas — depósito del 50% al reservar, resto el día de la salida. Qué hace especial cada yate de nuestra flota Astondoa 40 ("Fufi"): fabricado en Cádiz (Astondoa, astillero español de tradición), interior en teca y crema. Es nuestro yate más "mediterráneo" — perfecto para parejas y grupos pequeños que buscan estilo clásico. Capacidad 9 invitados, cocina equipada y dos camarotes para charter nocturno. Azimut 39: diseño italiano flybridge con líneas modernas. Es el yate ideal para grupos más numerosos (hasta 11 invitados) que quieren disfrutar tanto de la cubierta inferior como del puesto de mando superior con tumbonas al sol. Aire acondicionado, salón con vistas panorámicas y bañera amplia. Mangusta 80 ("Nina"): nuestra nave insignia. 24 metros de yate deportivo italiano de Overmarine, en flota desde 2020, con moto de agua Sea-Doo gratis para los invitados durante todo el día. Camarote principal, dos camarotes adicionales y galera con encimera para chef. Mínimo 4 horas, desde €4.719. Extras opcionales Comida a bordo: tapas (€25/persona), almuerzo completo caliente (€60/persona), platos sushi (€35/persona). Pedido con 24 h de antelación. Champán/bebidas premium: añade botellas específicas si prefieres marcas concretas. Nuestra cava de uso normal incluye cava español de calidad. DJ y sistema de sonido: +€350 medio día, +€600 día completo. Ideal para fiestas de cumpleaños o despedidas. Tender a chiringuito: te acercamos en lancha a Nikki Beach, Ocean Club o un beach club que prefieras. La entrada al club se paga aparte. Moto de agua extra: €250 la primera hora si quieres una segunda moto de agua (el Mangusta 80 ya incluye una). Preguntas frecuentes ¿Cuánto cuesta alquilar un yate en Marbella? El alquiler de yates en Marbella en nuestra flota arranca en €749 por 2 horas en el Astondoa 40 o Azimut 39 (12,5 m, hasta 11 invitados). Un día completo de 8 horas cuesta €2.299. Para el Mangusta 80 (24 m de lujo, con moto de agua), desde €4.719 por 4 horas. ¿Necesito licencia para alquilar? No. Todos nuestros yates van con patrón profesional incluido. Solo necesitas DNI o pasaporte para subir a bordo. ¿Cuántos invitados puedo llevar? El Astondoa 40 admite hasta 9 invitados, el Azimut 39 hasta 11, y el Mangusta 80 hasta 12. ¿Se puede pernoctar? Sí en yates de 12 m o más. El charter nocturno empieza en €1.200 y se cotiza por WhatsApp. Con nuestros clientes a bordo Charters reales con grupos reales en Marbella, Puerto Banús y la Costa del Sol. ---
URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/experiences/africa-from-marbella-by-boat/
TITLE: See Africa from Marbella by Boat: The Day-Trip Reality
DESCRIPTION: Can you see Africa from Marbella by boat? Yes — the Rif Mountains sit 75 NM south. Honest 10h day-trip reality + charter options. Ask on WhatsApp.

Home › See Africa from Marbella by Boat: The Day-Trip Reality Yes, you can see Africa from Marbella by boat — on a clear morning the Rif Mountains of northern Morocco rise across the Alborán Sea roughly 75 nautical miles to the south. What you can't do is sail there and back in a day. This page is the honest breakdown: what's actually visible from the water, which charter routes give the best African sightlines, and why the romantic idea of a day-trip to Tangier under sail doesn't survive contact with customs, fuel ranges, and the Levante wind. If you want African views with a glass of cava in hand, we'll show you how. If you want to set foot on African soil today, we'll point you to the ferry. The geography in three numbers Marbella sits on the northern shore of the Alborán Sea, the western basin of the Mediterranean. Three numbers matter for anyone asking whether Africa is reachable by boat: 75 NM — straight-line distance from Puerto Banús to the nearest Moroccan coast (Ceuta/Tangier area). 851m — height of Jebel Musa, the prominent African peak visible from Marbella on clear days. 14 NM — width of the Strait of Gibraltar at its narrowest point, west of Tarifa. The 75 NM figure is the critical one. At a cruising speed of 22-25 knots — typical for our Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 — that's three to three and a half hours one way. Round-trip with margin: a full 8-10 hour day burning fuel before you even step off the boat. What you can actually see on a clear day From a charter departing Puerto Banús, the African horizon becomes visible once you're 2-3 NM offshore and the coastal heat haze drops away. On the cleanest days — typically after rain or during a steady Poniente westerly — you'll see: The Rif mountain range as a long ridgeline along the southern horizon Jebel Musa as a distinct triangular peak, one of the two classical Pillars of Hercules The Rock of Gibraltar to the west, often more prominent than Africa itself The Spanish enclave of Ceuta as a thin smudge of land below the mountains Binoculars help. So does altitude — the flybridge of an Azimut 58 gives you a measurably better sightline than a deck-level cockpit. Bring polarised sunglasses to cut Mediterranean glare. Best months and times for African visibility Visibility from Marbella across the Alborán is dictated by humidity, wind direction, and Saharan dust. The pattern is consistent year to year: Period African visibility Notes April-June Excellent Long mornings, low haze, calm seas July-August Moderate Heat haze + occasional Levante dust September-October Excellent Clearest light of the year November-March Variable Sharp after rain, rough sea state Always book a morning slot if Africa is on your wish-list. Departures between 9am and 11am give you 3-4 hours of clear horizon before afternoon haze builds. By 3pm in summer the coastline often disappears even if the morning was crystal clear. Why we don't run day-trips to Morocco Clients regularly ask if we can charter to Tangier for lunch. The answer is no, and here's the full reasoning rather than a vague brush-off: Customs clearance — exiting Schengen by private vessel requires advance Guardia Civil paperwork. Moroccan entry processing in Tangier adds 60-90 minutes per direction. Weather margin — the Strait of Gibraltar is one of the windiest stretches in Europe. Levante easterlies can build from 10 to 30+ knots within hours. You need a confirmed 12-hour weather window. Fuel range — most charter motoryachts can complete the round-trip, but at the cost of cruising slowly and arriving with minimal reserve. Not a sensible day-trip profile. Insurance — our coverage is regional. Cross-border crossings require dedicated policy extensions and licensed offshore crews. If Africa as a destination is the goal, drive an hour west to Tarifa and take the FRS or Inter Shipping fast ferry — it crosses to Tangier-Ville in 60 minutes and costs around €40 each way. The best charter routes for African views If you want African horizon views from a private boat, the route matters more than the boat. Three options from our fleet , ranked by Africa-visibility payoff: 1. Puerto Banús → Estepona → Punta Chullera (8 hours) The westbound run takes you progressively closer to the strait. By Punta Chullera, near the Sotogrande border, you're 60 NM from the African coast with Jebel Musa squarely on the southern horizon. A full-day Astondoa 40 charter at €2,299 covers this comfortably with two swim stops. 2. Puerto Banús → Cabopino → Marbella east (4 hours) Eastbound runs trade African visibility for the dramatic backdrop of La Concha mountain. Africa is still visible but at a steeper angle. Good for guests who prioritise coastal scenery over the strait. 3. Mangusta 80 sunset cruise to Estepona (4 hours minimum) The Mangusta 80 at €4,719 includes a free jet ski and runs a sunset-direction route that puts the African coast in silhouette during golden hour. Photographically the best Africa-view charter we offer. What's included on every charter Whichever route you choose, every booking covers the full experience without bolt-on surprises: Licensed Spanish skipper with local Alborán knowledge Fuel for the agreed route Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava Light snacks (tapas-style platters) Full insurance and safety equipment Spanish IVA (21%) — no hidden tax on your final bill What's not included: lunch ashore at Estepona or Sotogrande, jet ski fuel beyond the included allowance on the Mangusta 80, and any Moroccan landing fees (which is academic given we don't cross). Booking the right boat for Africa views For two to nine guests prioritising clear horizon views, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 are the natural pick — the cruising speed gets you west to Sotogrande waters within 90 minutes, leaving plenty of time for swim stops and sightseeing. For groups of 10-12, the Azimut 58 Flybridge gives you the deck height that genuinely improves the sightline across 75 NM of sea. Larger groups or special occasions step up to the Mangusta 80 , where the included jet ski adds a memorable detour on the way back to Puerto Banús. Whichever you choose, message us on WhatsApp with your date and group size — we'll confirm a morning slot that gives the best Africa visibility for that week's weather pattern. Frequently asked questions Can you actually see Africa from Marbella? Yes, on clear days. The Rif Mountains of northern Morocco — particularly Jebel Musa at 851m — are visible from Marbella's higher viewpoints like La Concha and from boats a few miles offshore. Visibility is best in spring and autumn mornings when humidity is low and the Levante wind hasn't kicked up haze. From sea level you typically need to be 3-5 NM offshore for a clean sightline across the 75 NM gap. How far is Africa from Marbella by boat? Roughly 75 nautical miles (140 km) south to the nearest Moroccan coast near Ceuta or Tangier. At a comfortable cruising speed of 22-25 knots, a one-way crossing takes 3 to 3.5 hours in calm conditions. Round-trip that's 6-7 hours of pure navigation, before you factor in customs, fuel, and weather margin — which is why we don't run it as a day charter. Can I do a same-day boat trip from Marbella to Morocco? Not realistically on a private charter. The crossing requires Schengen exit clearance, Moroccan customs entry, and a weather window that holds for 10+ hours. Most clients who want to set foot in Africa drive 1h to Tarifa and take the 1-hour fast ferry to Tangier instead. For boat-based Africa views, we recommend a sunset charter from Puerto Banús toward Estepona — the African coastline is clearest at golden hour. Which Marbella boat charter gets closest to Africa? Sotogrande and Estepona-direction routes put you 5-8 NM closer to the African coast than Marbella centre. From a Tier A charter like the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, a 4-hour westbound run reaches the waters off Manilva with Jebel Musa clearly visible to the south on a clear day. For longer-range visibility a Man

TITLE: Baby Shower Yacht Charter Marbella: Calm Day on the Bay
DESCRIPTION: Plan a baby shower yacht Marbella charter for a calm, private day on the bay. From €749/2h with skipper, drinks &amp; snacks included. Book now.

Home › Baby Shower Yacht Charter Marbella: Calm Day on the Bay Celebrate your baby shower on a private yacht charter from Puerto Banús, Marbella. A calm day on the bay with soft drinks, snacks, and a licensed skipper means you can relax and enjoy the moment. Choose from our fleet of luxury boats, all departing from the iconic marina, and anchor in sheltered spots like Cala del Faro or off the Golden Mile. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours, including everything except decorations. No hidden costs, no stress – just a beautiful afternoon on the water. Why a Baby Shower Yacht in Marbella? Marbella’s coastline offers calm waters, warm sunshine, and stunning views of La Concha mountain. A private yacht gives you a peaceful, exclusive setting away from crowded restaurants. You control the itinerary, the music, and the pace. The bay is sheltered, so even guests who are not keen on boats will feel comfortable. With a licensed skipper handling navigation, you can focus on celebrating. Our Fleet for Baby Showers We have boats to suit groups of 5 to 12 people. For an intimate baby shower, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are ideal. Both are spacious, with shaded aft decks and comfortable seating. Larger groups can book the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) from €4,719 for 4 hours, which includes a free jet ski. All boats depart from Puerto Banús and are fully equipped with safety gear, sound systems, and onboard facilities. What’s Included in Your Charter Licensed skipper (English & Spanish speaking) Fuel for the full charter duration Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava Light snacks (chips, olives, nuts) Insurance and safety equipment Spanish IVA (21%) – no surprises You are welcome to bring your own cake, decorations, or additional food. Let us know in advance and we can provide a table and help set up. Sample Itinerary: 4-Hour Baby Shower Cruise A typical 4-hour baby shower charter might look like this: 10:30 – Meet at Puerto Banús, board your yacht 10:45 – Depart, head east towards Marbella Marina 11:15 – Anchor at Cala del Faro for swimming and photos 12:00 – Serve cava and snacks, enjoy the calm bay 13:00 – Cruise back along the Golden Mile 14:00 – Return to Puerto Banús We can adjust the route to suit your preferences – longer stops, different anchorages, or a sunset timing. Pricing for Baby Shower Yacht Charters Boat Duration Price (all inclusive) Astondoa 40 / Azimut 39 2 hours €749 Astondoa 40 / Azimut 39 4 hours €1,299 Astondoa 40 / Azimut 39 8 hours €2,299 Mangusta 80 (any variant) 4 hours minimum €4,719 (includes free jet ski) Other boats (Ferretti 94, Pershing 46, etc.) Various Pricing on request via WhatsApp All prices include skipper, drinks, snacks, fuel, insurance, and VAT. No extra fees. Best Anchorages for a Calm Baby Shower The Marbella coast has several sheltered spots perfect for a relaxed celebration: Cala del Faro – A quiet cove east of Marbella, protected from swell, with clear water. Off the Golden Mile – Calm waters with views of luxury villas and La Concha. Cabopino – A sandy beach with shallow waters, good for swimming. Puerto Banús entrance – Close to the marina, calm and convenient for short charters. Your skipper will choose the best spot based on wind and sea conditions. How to Book Your Baby Shower Yacht Booking is straightforward. Contact us via WhatsApp or the Boat Rental Marbella website with your preferred date, group size, and any special requests. We will confirm availability and send a secure payment link. A 50% deposit secures the booking, with the balance due 7 days before departure. We offer free cancellation up to 48 hours before departure, and full refunds if weather forces cancellation. Frequently asked questions What is included in a baby shower yacht charter in Marbella? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. You only need to bring decorations or extra food if desired – we can arrange catering on request. Which boat is best for a baby shower on the bay? For a calm, intimate baby shower, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, up to 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, up to 11 pax) are ideal. Both depart from Puerto Banús and offer comfortable seating, shade, and easy access to calm anchorages. How much does a baby shower yacht charter cost in Marbella? Prices start from €749 for 2 hours on Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) up to €2,299 for 8 hours. Larger boats like the Mangusta 80 cost from €4,719 for 4 hours. All prices include skipper, drinks, and snacks. Can we bring decorations or a cake on board? Yes, you are welcome to bring decorations, a cake, or any personal items. We recommend lightweight, non-messy decorations. Our crew can help set up a small table. Please let us know in advance so we can assist. Where do baby shower charters depart from? All our charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. The marina is easy to reach by car or taxi, with ample parking nearby. We meet you at the boat 15 minutes before departure. Is the sea calm enough for a baby shower? Yes, the bay of Marbella is generally very calm, especially in the morning. Our skippers choose sheltered anchorages like Cala del Faro or off the Golden Mile to ensure a smooth, relaxing experience. Can we cancel or reschedule if the weather is bad? Yes, we offer free rescheduling or a full refund if the skipper deems conditions unsafe. We monitor forecasts closely and will contact you 24 hours before departure if changes are needed. How do I book a baby shower yacht in Marbella? Booking is easy: contact us via WhatsApp or our website. Provide your preferred date, group size, and any special requests. We will confirm availability and send a payment link. URL: https://boatrentalinmarbella.com/experiences/big-game-tuna-fishing-marbella/
TITLE: Big Game Tuna Fishing Charter Marbella
DESCRIPTION: Book a big game tuna fishing charter in Marbella. Private boat, licensed skipper, gear &amp; drinks included. Catch bluefin off Puerto Banús.

Home › Big Game Tuna Fishing Charter Marbella Marbella's waters are a prime destination for big game tuna fishing . The Alboran Sea, just off the Costa del Sol, hosts bluefin tuna runs from June to October, with fish often exceeding 50 kg. Our private charters depart from Puerto Banús and include a licensed skipper, all fishing gear, bait, drinks, and snacks. Whether you're an experienced angler or a first-timer, our boats offer a comfortable, professional platform for a day on the water. Why Marbella for Big Game Tuna Fishing? The Mediterranean's bluefin tuna migrate through the Strait of Gibraltar into the Alboran Sea, making Marbella a strategic launch point. Water temperatures in summer reach 24–26°C, and the continental shelf drops quickly, bringing tuna within 5–10 nautical miles of the coast. The season peaks from July to September, when catches of 60–100 kg are common. Local skippers know the hotspots near Cabopino, Cala del Faro, and Estepona. Our Fishing Boats We offer three boat types for tuna charters, all departing from Puerto Banús: Astondoa 40 – 12.5m, up to 9 passengers. Ideal for small groups. From €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. Azimut 39 – 12.5m, up to 11 passengers. Spacious cockpit for fighting fish. Same pricing as Astondoa 40. Mangusta 80 – 24m, up to 12 passengers. Premium experience with more deck space. Pricing on request via WhatsApp. All boats are equipped with outriggers, fighting chairs, and heavy-duty rods and reels. The skipper handles all navigation and fishing logistics. What’s Included Every charter includes: licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, fishing gear, bait, and tackle. The Spanish IVA (21%) is included in the price. No hidden extras. You can also browse all our Marbella boat rentals for other experiences. Typical Day on the Water Your charter starts at Puerto Banús marina. After a safety briefing, the skipper heads out to the fishing grounds, usually 5–15 nautical miles offshore. Trolling with live bait or lures is the standard technique. The skipper will rig the lines and coach you on fighting the fish. Catches are cleaned and packed on request. You can also swim or relax between action. Most charters last 4–8 hours, but shorter 2-hour trips are available for a taste of the sport. Season & Conditions Bluefin tuna season runs June–October. Peak months are July, August, and September. Early morning departures (7–8 AM) often yield the best action. Sea conditions are generally calm in summer, with swells under 1 m. If the wind picks up, the skipper may adjust the route or reschedule. We recommend booking in advance during peak season as demand is high. Booking & Pricing For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), prices are fixed: €749 for 2 hours, €1,299 for 4 hours, €1,799 for 6 hours, and €2,299 for 8 hours. For the Mangusta 80 or other boats, contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote. A 50% deposit secures your booking. Cancellations made 48 hours before departure receive a full refund. For more details on our fleet, see our full boat selection . Frequently asked questions What is included in a big game tuna fishing charter in Marbella? Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. Fishing gear, bait, and tackle are also included. The Spanish IVA (21%) is included in the price. Which boats are available for tuna fishing? We offer the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax), Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax), and Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) for fishing charters. All depart from Puerto Banús. Other boats may be available on request. Do I need a fishing license? No. The charter includes a temporary fishing license for all passengers, arranged by the skipper. You just show up and fish. What is the best time of year for big game tuna fishing in Marbella? Bluefin tuna season runs from June to October, with peak catches from July through September. Water temperatures then reach 22–26°C, bringing larger fish closer to the coast. How far do we go from the coast? Depending on conditions and tuna location, you may travel 5–15 nautical miles from Marbella. The skipper will head to known fishing grounds in the Alboran Sea, often near Cabopino or Estepona. Can we keep the fish we catch? Yes. The skipper can help clean and pack your catch for transport. Note that there are legal size limits and quotas for bluefin tuna, which the skipper will ensure are respected. What size of tuna can we expect? Bluefin tuna in the Alboran Sea typically range from 30 kg to over 100 kg. Catches of 50–80 kg are common during peak season. The gear provided is heavy-duty to handle these fish. How do I book a big game tuna fishing charter? Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), prices start at €749 for 2 hours and €2,299 for 8 hours. Larger boats like the Mangusta 80 are priced on request. ★★★★★ 4.9 · verified reviews From €749 2h skippered charter Instant quotes from local operators across Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona & Sotogrande. 💬 Message on WhatsApp Avg reply < 5 min · No deposit until you confirm 📅 Book now Browse the full fleet → All charters include Licensed skipper & fuel Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava Light snacks Insurance & safety equipment Snorkel gear & inflatables VAT 21% — no surprises
